You are auditing source code for an INDEPENDENT external code review (client: Telus). Tools: Bash, Read.

STEP 0 — SKIP IF DONE. Run: test -s /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_66.tsv && echo DONE || echo TODO
If it prints DONE, this shard is already complete — reply exactly "shard 66: cached" and STOP. Do nothing else.

STEP 1 — get your file list: /opt/conda/bin/python -c "import json; print(chr(10).join(json.load(open('/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.name_shards.json'))[66]))"
It prints lines "relpath:START-END" relative to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent.

STEP 2 — GENUINELY READ every assigned file/range line by line with the Read tool (offset/limit from START-END; use limit up to 2000 per call to minimize calls). Do NOT grep-and-skim — read the code.

STEP 3 — flag EVERY name (file/module, class, func/method, var, const, enum value) that looks UNPROFESSIONAL in an external review:
  - marketing/quality adjectives: honest, real, true, smart, magic, ultimate, enhanced, improved, robust, aggressive, killer, awesome, blazing, super, mega, best
  - temporal/version markers: v2/v3/v4, r2/r3, _new, _old, legacy, deprecated, next/nextgen, final(meaning 'this version'), fixed, temp, wip, draft, backup, orig, working, experimental, proto
  - jokes/informal/vague/slang: junk, garbage, hack, spy, stub(as a real name), yolo, stuff, misc, foo/bar, doit, thing, blah, landmine, nuke, magic-number-as-word, terse cryptic abbreviations
Judge at a STRICT bar: **when in doubt, mark RENAME.** Only KEEP-BUT-NOTED for unambiguously standard, descriptive, professional vocabulary or a REAL domain term genuinely implemented (finalize, proper_noun, evict_older_than, SmartArt only if it truly makes SmartArt).

STEP 4 — APPEND TAB-separated rows to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_66.tsv (one per flagged name), using printf with real tabs:
  VERDICT<TAB>NAME<TAB>relpath:LINE<TAB>KIND<TAB>WHY<TAB>SUGGESTED_NAME
  VERDICT=RENAME or KEEP-NOTED; KIND=file/class/func/var/const/enum. If a file is fully clean, write: NONE<TAB>-<TAB>relpath<TAB>-<TAB>clean<TAB>-

Reply ONLY: "shard 66: <R> renames, <K> kept across <F> files".

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	"""
2	Section-by-section report writer for polaris graph.
3	
4	Each section is a separate LLM call, keeping output under 800 words
5	to stay within the LLM's quality sweet spot.
6	
7	No single mega-call. No CoT leakage. No post-processing.
8	"""
9	
10	import asyncio
11	import logging
12	import os
13	import re
14	from typing import Optional
15	
16	import numpy as np
17	
18	from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient
19	from src.polaris_graph.schemas import (
20	    QuestionDecomposition,
21	    ReportOutline,
22	    SectionDraft,
23	    SectionOutlineItem,
24	)
25	from src.polaris_graph.state import (
26	    EvidencePiece,
27	    MAX_WORDS_PER_SECTION,
28	    PG_SECTION_CONTINUATION_MAX_TOKENS,
29	    PG_SECTION_EVIDENCE_TOP_K,
30	    PG_SECTION_WRITER_MAX_TOKENS,
31	    PG_SYNTHESIS_STRUCTURED_MAX_TOKENS,
32	    ReportSection,
33	)
34	from src.polaris_graph.tracing import get_tracer
35	
36	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
37	
38	SECTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT = """You are writing one section of a research report. You have {n_evidence} evidence pieces.
39	
40	GENERATION RULE: Write ONLY from the evidence provided. For every factual claim, cite
41	the evidence ID immediately after: [CITE:evidence_id]. If you cannot cite a claim, do not write it.
42	
43	STRUCTURE RULES:
44	1. If your evidence contains 4+ comparable data points (same measurement, different entities),
45	   present them as a MARKDOWN TABLE with citations in each cell.
46	2. If your evidence contains time-series data or trends, describe them quantitatively.
47	3. End each section with a **Key Findings** subsection: 3-5 cited bullet points summarizing
48	   the claims a reader should remember from this section.
49	4. Write analytically: comparisons, trade-offs, implications — not just facts.
50	
51	CITATION RULES:
52	5. Every factual claim MUST have [CITE:evidence_id] immediately after.
53	6. Include exact numbers, dates, measurements from evidence. NEVER invent numbers.
54	7. STRONGLY prefer evidence marked [VERIFIED] over [UNVERIFIED].
55	8. Do NOT cite the same source more than 3 times per section. Spread across sources.
56	9. If you CANNOT cite a number, use qualitative language ("evidence suggests") instead.
57	10. Use EXACTLY the units from the source. Never convert or paraphrase units.
58	
59	FIX-HALLUC-1 — ABSOLUTE HALLUCINATION BAN (read carefully):
60	  Use ONLY the evidence provided above. Do NOT use training knowledge, do NOT recall
61	  studies, papers, PMIDs, author names, sample sizes, regulatory bodies, or quoted
62	  expert reactions unless the specific fact is supported by an evidence piece shown
63	  to you AND tagged with [CITE:evidence_id]. If you feel tempted to write a specific
64	  fact you "know" but it is not in the evidence: DROP IT. Under NO circumstance invent
65	  or recall: PMIDs, DOIs, author names (e.g., "Zhong et al."), sample sizes (e.g., "20,078 adults"),
66	  follow-up durations, percent risk ratios, study locations, cohort names, regulatory agency
67	  positions (FDA/EFSA/EMA/FTC/Health Canada), media outlets (e.g., "Science Media Centre"),
68	  or expert critiques. If the prose needs such a fact and no evidence supports it, write a
69	  neutral qualitative sentence instead (e.g., "observational data have raised long-term
70	  safety questions") without specifics. Sections containing uncited specific numbers or
71	  uncited named entities WILL BE REJECTED by the audit.
72	
73	FIX-6 — INTERPRETIVE OVERLAY BAN:
74	  Uncited sentences are allowed ONLY as neutral connectives between two
75	  [CITE:evidence_id]-anchored sentences. The following are FORBIDDEN without a
76	  supporting [CITE:]:
77	    * Clinical recommendations: "clinical translation should...", "clinicians
78	      should therefore...", "practitioners are advised to..."
79	    * Qualifier overlays: "the most robust signal is...", "the most important
80	      finding is...", "the best-studied protocol is..."
81	    * Synthesis overlays: "taken together...", "integrating these threads
82	      suggests...", "the evidence supports an X framing rather than Y..."
83	    * Editorial framings: "this framing provides public-health context but...",
84	      "adherence is the first-order mechanism...", "research priorities are..."
85	    * Fabricated taxonomies not attributable to a specific cited source.
86	  If you cannot cite a claim, do NOT write it. "Taken together" is a banned
87	  sentence opener unless the sentence that follows carries a [CITE:].
88	
89	FIX-6 — TITLE-ONLY BAN:
90	  Do NOT pad sections with standalone sentences that only restate a source's
91	  title or scope (e.g. "A review of X examines whether Y [CITE:ev_xxx]"). Every
92	  sentence must report a specific finding, number, or causal link — not merely
93	  that a source exists. Thin atomic facts must be integrated into comparative
94	  paragraphs or dropped.
95	
96	FIX-6 — CHERRY-PICK PREVENTION:
97	  When citing a meta-analysis or review with multiple pooled outcomes, either
98	  (a) report all primary outcomes the source analyzed, or (b) note explicitly
99	  that you are reporting one outcome among several. Do NOT present a single
100	  statistically significant finding from a predominantly-null study as if it
101	  were the headline result.
102	
103	FORWARD-PROMISE RULE: Do NOT enumerate topics the report "will cover" unless you will
104	  actually cover them in this section. Do not introduce axes, frameworks, or themes that
105	  require content beyond what your evidence supports.
106	
107	STYLE RULES:
108	11. Write in third person, academic register. No meta-commentary or preamble.
109	12. Use hedging words (may, might, could) ONLY for genuinely uncertain findings. Max 5 per section.
110	13. Use transition words SPARINGLY — max 1 per 200 words. NEVER start consecutive sentences with them.
111	14. Do NOT include chain-of-thought, reasoning, or planning text.
112	15. Do NOT repeat the section title in the content.
113	16. Do NOT repeat statistics that appeared in previous sections.
114	17. When citing regulatory thresholds, note when they were established.
115	
116	LENGTH: Write proportional to evidence depth. {n_evidence} pieces ~ {suggested_words} words.
117	Do NOT pad beyond what evidence supports. Quality over quantity."""
118	
119	OUTLINE_SYSTEM_PROMPT = """You are a research report architect. Design report structure FROM evidence, not templates.
120	
121	Given evidence clusters with viability assessments, create a data-driven outline where:
122	- Each section EXISTS because evidence supports it (not because a template demands it)
123	- Section depth is PROPORTIONAL to evidence depth (more evidence = more detailed section)
124	- Clusters with contradictory evidence get balanced treatment sections
125	- Clusters assessed as BRIEF become bullet points in an "Additional Findings" section
126	
127	Requirements:
128	1. Each section should have a clear, specific title (not generic like "Introduction").
129	2. Sections should flow logically from background to findings to implications.
130	3. Evidence will be assigned to sections automatically — focus on designing the best structure.
131	4. Each section's target_words should be proportional to its evidence: min(200 + n_evidence * 80, 2000).
132	5. Include a brief abstract (150-250 words) summarizing key findings.
133	6. For each section, state which cluster numbers it draws from.
134	7. No two sections should cover the same subtopic. Each section must have a unique analytical angle.
135	8. The abstract should NOT include specific numeric claims about source counts or evidence quantities.
136	9. FIX-MP18: The abstract MUST include: (a) scope, (b) exclusions, (c) evidence limitations.
137	10. If evidence contains comparable data points, note which sections should include TABLES.
138	11. Do NOT pad. A section with 3 evidence pieces warrants a concise paragraph, not 800 words.
139	
140	Output format example:
141	{"title": "Comprehensive Analysis of X: Mechanisms, Impacts, and Regulatory Landscape", "abstract": "This report examines...", "sections": [{"section_id": "s01", "title": "Historical Context and Emergence of X", "description": "Traces the discovery and early research. Covers clusters 1, 2. Contains comparison data suitable for TABLE.", "evidence_ids": [], "target_words": 600, "order": 1}], "total_target_words": 6000}"""
142	
143	
144	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
145	# Phase 5: Modular Prompt Fragments (Claw Code 160+ fragment architecture)
146	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
147	
148	_PROMPT_FRAGMENT_DIR = os.path.join(
149	    os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))),
150	    "config", "prompts",
151	)
152	
153	
154	def _detect_section_type(evidence_items: list[dict]) -> str:
155	    """Detect section type from evidence CHARACTERISTICS, not title.
156	
157	    Returns one of: comparison, safety, mechanism, methodology, clinical (default).
158	    """
159	    if not evidence_items:
160	        return "clinical"
161	
162	    statements = " ".join(
163	        e.get("statement", "") for e in evidence_items[:20]
164	    ).lower()
165	
166	    # Count keyword signals per type
167	    comparison_kw = sum(1 for kw in [
168	        "compared to", "versus", "vs ", "outperformed", "superior",
169	        "inferior", "non-inferior", "effect size", "odds ratio",
170	        "relative risk", "hazard ratio", "confidence interval",
171	    ] if kw in statements)
172	
173	    safety_kw = sum(1 for kw in [
174	        "adverse", "risk", "contraindication", "side effect", "toxicity",
175	        "mortality", "morbidity", "withdrawal", "discontinuation",
176	        "safety", "harm", "dangerous", "fatal",
177	    ] if kw in statements)
178	
179	    mechanism_kw = sum(1 for kw in [
180	        "mechanism", "pathway", "receptor", "enzyme", "autophagy",
181	        "signaling", "metabolic", "molecular", "cellular", "binding",
182	        "expression", "phosphorylation", "inhibition", "activation",
183	    ] if kw in statements)
184	
185	    methodology_kw = sum(1 for kw in [
186	        "meta-analysis", "systematic review", "grade", "bias",
187	        "heterogeneity", "sample size", "power", "blinding",
188	        "randomization", "funnel plot", "i-squared", "cochrane",
189	    ] if kw in statements)
190	
191	    scores = {
192	        "comparison": comparison_kw,
193	        "safety": safety_kw,
194	        "mechanism": mechanism_kw,
195	        "methodology": methodology_kw,
196	    }
197	
198	    best_type = max(scores, key=scores.get)
199	    if scores[best_type] >= 2:
200	        return best_type
201	    return "clinical"
202	
203	
204	def _load_prompt_fragment(section_type: str) -> str:
205	    """Load a prompt fragment for the detected section type.
206	
207	    Returns fragment text, or empty string if file not found.
208	    Max 300 tokens (~1200 chars) enforced.
209	    """
210	    _max_chars = int(os.getenv("PG_PROMPT_FRAGMENT_MAX_CHARS", "1200"))
211	    fragment_path = os.path.join(_PROMPT_FRAGMENT_DIR, f"fragment_{section_type}.md")
212	
213	    try:
214	        with open(fragment_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
215	            content = f.read().strip()
216	        if len(content) > _max_chars:
217	            content = content[:_max_chars].rsplit("\n", 1)[0]
218	        return content
219	    except FileNotFoundError:
220	        logger.debug(
221	            "[polaris graph] Phase 5: Fragment file not found: %s",
222	            fragment_path,
223	        )
224	        return ""
225	    except Exception as exc:
226	        logger.warning(
227	            "[polaris graph] Phase 5: Failed to load fragment '%s': %s",
228	            section_type, str(exc)[:200],
229	        )
230	        return ""
231	
232	
233	def _load_base_rules() -> str:
234	    """Load the base rules prompt fragment."""
235	    base_path = os.path.join(_PROMPT_FRAGMENT_DIR, "base_rules.md")
236	    try:
237	        with open(base_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
238	            return f.read().strip()
239	    except Exception:
240	        return ""
241	
242	
243	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
244	# FIX-107I: Per-section evidence filtering via embedding similarity
245	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
246	
247	def _filter_evidence_for_section(
248	    evidence: list[EvidencePiece],
249	    section_title: str,
250	    section_description: str,
251	    top_k: int = 100,
252	) -> list[EvidencePiece]:
253	    """FIX-107I: Filter evidence by embedding similarity to section topic.
254	
255	    Before writing each section, compute cosine similarity between the
256	    section topic (title + description) and every evidence statement.
257	    Return the top_k most similar pieces so the LLM receives focused,
258	    relevant evidence instead of the full pool.
259	
260	    Graceful fallback: if embedding fails for any reason, returns the
261	    full evidence list unchanged (no silent degradation of capability,
262	    just skips the optimization).
263	
264	    Args:
265	        evidence: Full pool of evidence pieces for the section (already
266	            filtered to outline-assigned IDs).
267	        section_title: The section's title string.
268	        section_description: The section's description string.
269	        top_k: Maximum number of evidence pieces to return.
270	
271	    Returns:
272	        Filtered list of at most ``top_k`` evidence pieces, sorted by
273	        descending similarity to the section topic.
274	    """
275	    # If evidence pool is already small enough, skip embedding entirely
276	    if len(evidence) <= top_k:
277	        logger.debug(
278	            "[polaris graph] FIX-107I: Section '%s' has %d evidence (<= top_k=%d), "
279	            "skipping filtering",
280	            section_title[:50],
281	            len(evidence),
282	            top_k,
283	        )
284	        return evidence
285	
286	    try:
287	        from src.utils.embedding_service import embed_text, embed_texts
288	    except ImportError:
289	        logger.warning(
290	            "[polaris graph] FIX-107I: embedding_service unavailable, "
291	            "skipping per-section evidence filtering for '%s'",
292	            section_title[:50],
293	        )
294	        return evidence
295	
296	    try:
297	        # Build query from section title + description
298	        query_text = f"{section_title}. {section_description}"
299	        query_vec = np.array(embed_text(query_text))
300	
301	        # Batch-embed all evidence statements
302	        statements = [e.get("statement", "") for e in evidence]
303	        statement_vecs = np.array(embed_texts(statements))
304	
305	        # Cosine similarity (embeddings are pre-normalized -> dot product)
306	        similarities = statement_vecs @ query_vec
307	
308	        # Get indices of top_k most similar evidence pieces
309	        # np.argpartition is O(n) vs O(n log n) for argsort, but we need
310	        # sorted output for deterministic ordering, so use argsort on the
311	        # partitioned subset.
312	        if len(similarities) > top_k:
313	            top_indices = np.argsort(similarities)[-top_k:][::-1]
314	        else:
315	            top_indices = np.argsort(similarities)[::-1]
316	
317	        filtered = [evidence[i] for i in top_indices]
318	
319	        # Log the filtering result
320	        kept_ids = [e.get("evidence_id", "?") for e in filtered]
321	        min_sim = float(similarities[top_indices[-1]]) if len(top_indices) > 0 else 0.0
322	        max_sim = float(similarities[top_indices[0]]) if len(top_indices) > 0 else 0.0
323	
324	        logger.info(
325	            "[polaris graph] FIX-107I: Section '%s' filtered %d -> %d evidence pieces "
326	            "(sim range %.3f-%.3f)",
327	            section_title[:50],
328	            len(evidence),
329	            len(filtered),
330	            min_sim,
331	            max_sim,
332	        )
333	
334	        return filtered
335	
336	    except Exception as exc:
337	        # Graceful fallback: return all evidence if embedding fails
338	        logger.warning(
339	            "[polaris graph] FIX-107I: Embedding failed for section '%s': %s — "
340	            "returning all %d evidence pieces unfiltered",
341	            section_title[:50],
342	            str(exc)[:200],
343	            len(evidence),
344	        )
345	        return evidence
346	
347	
348	def _build_comparison_tables(
349	    section_evidence: list[dict],
350	) -> str:
351	    """RC-6: Build pre-formatted markdown tables from comparable_metrics.
352	
353	    Groups metrics by metric_name across evidence pieces. For groups with
354	    3+ data points, generates markdown tables with [CITE:evidence_id] in
355	    the Source column.
356	
357	    Returns formatted table block string, or empty string if no tables.
358	    """
359	    from collections import defaultdict
360	
361	    # Collect all comparable_metrics from evidence cards
362	    metric_groups: dict[str, list[tuple[dict, str]]] = defaultdict(list)
363	    for ev in section_evidence:
364	        metrics = ev.get("comparable_metrics") or []
365	        ev_id = ev.get("evidence_id", "?")
366	        for m in metrics:
367	            if isinstance(m, dict) and m.get("metric_name") and m.get("value") is not None:
368	                metric_groups[m["metric_name"]].append((m, ev_id))
369	
370	    if not metric_groups:
371	        return ""
372	
373	    tables = []
374	    for metric_name, entries in sorted(metric_groups.items()):
375	        if len(entries) < 3:
376	            continue
377	
378	        # Build markdown table
379	        table_lines = [
380	            f"\n**Comparison: {metric_name.replace('_', ' ').title()}**\n",
381	            "| Source | Entity | Value | Unit | Condition |",
382	            "|--------|--------|-------|------|-----------|",
383	        ]
384	        for m, ev_id in entries[:15]:  # Cap at 15 rows
385	            entity = m.get("entity", "")
386	            value = m.get("value", "")
387	            unit = m.get("unit", "")
388	            condition = m.get("condition", "")
389	            table_lines.append(f"| [CITE:{ev_id}] | {entity} | {value} | {unit} | {condition} |")
390	
391	        tables.append("\n".join(table_lines))
392	
393	    if not tables:
394	        return ""
395	
396	    return "\n\n".join(tables)
397	
398	
399	def _format_conflicts_for_prompt(
400	    evidence_conflicts: list[dict],
401	    section_evidence_ids: list[str],
402	) -> str:
403	    """FIX-ENV4: Format evidence conflicts relevant to a section for inclusion in prompts.
404	
405	    Filters the global conflict list to only those conflicts where at least
406	    one evidence piece is assigned to this section. Returns a formatted
407	    string block for injection into LLM prompts.
408	
409	    Args:
410	        evidence_conflicts: Full list of detected conflict dicts.
411	        section_evidence_ids: Evidence IDs assigned to this section.
412	
413	    Returns:
414	        Formatted conflict summary string, or empty string if no relevant conflicts.
415	    """
416	    if not evidence_conflicts or not section_evidence_ids:
417	        return ""
418	
419	    section_ids = set(section_evidence_ids)
420	    relevant = []
421	    for conflict in evidence_conflicts:
422	        a_id = conflict.get("evidence_a_id", "")
423	        b_id = conflict.get("evidence_b_id", "")
424	        if a_id in section_ids or b_id in section_ids:
425	            relevant.append(conflict)
426	
427	    if not relevant:
428	        return ""
429	
430	    lines = [
431	        f"\nCONFLICTING EVIDENCE ({len(relevant)} conflicts detected in this section's evidence):",
432	        "You MUST dedicate at least one full paragraph to each conflict listed below.",
433	        "Structure: [Position A with citation] vs [Position B with citation].",
434	        "Then explain which position has stronger evidence and why.",
435	        "Do NOT merely mention the conflict — ANALYZE it with specific reasoning.",
436	    ]
437	    for i, conflict in enumerate(relevant[:5], 1):
438	        lines.append(
439	            f"  Conflict {i}: [{conflict.get('evidence_a_id', '?')}] vs [{conflict.get('evidence_b_id', '?')}]"
440	        )
441	        lines.append(
442	            f"    A: {conflict.get('statement_a', '')[:150]}"
443	        )
444	        lines.append(
445	            f"    B: {conflict.get('statement_b', '')[:150]}"
446	        )
447	        signals = conflict.get("contradiction_signals", [])
448	        if signals:
449	            lines.append(
450	                f"    Signals: {', '.join(signals[:3])}"
451	            )
452	
453	    return "\n".join(lines)
454

456	def _summarize_evidence_for_planning(
457	    clusters: list[dict],
458	    evidence: list[EvidencePiece],
459	) -> str:
460	    """Build a compact evidence summary for question decomposition (RC-3).
461	
462	    Returns a short text describing what themes and data points are available
463	    so the decomposition LLM can generate answerable sub-questions.
464	    """
465	    lines = [f"Total evidence pieces: {len(evidence)}"]
466	    gold = sum(1 for e in evidence if e.get("quality_tier") == "GOLD")
467	    silver = sum(1 for e in evidence if e.get("quality_tier") == "SILVER")
468	    lines.append(f"Quality: {gold} GOLD, {silver} SILVER, {len(evidence) - gold - silver} BRONZE")
469	
470	    perspectives: dict[str, int] = {}
471	    for e in evidence:
472	        p = e.get("perspective", "Unknown")
473	        perspectives[p] = perspectives.get(p, 0) + 1
474	    if perspectives:
475	        lines.append("Perspectives: " + ", ".join(
476	            f"{k} ({v})" for k, v in sorted(perspectives.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1])[:6]
477	        ))
478	
479	    for i, cl in enumerate(clusters[:12], 1):
480	        theme = cl.get("theme", cl.get("label", f"Cluster {i}"))
481	        count = len(cl.get("evidence_ids", []))
482	        lines.append(f"  Cluster {i}: {theme} ({count} evidence)")
483	
484	    return "\n".join(lines)
485	
486	
487	_QUESTION_DECOMPOSITION_SYSTEM = """You are a research strategist. Given a research topic and a summary of evidence collected, decompose the topic into 6-10 sub-questions that a knowledgeable reader would want answered.
488	
489	Requirements:
490	- Questions should flow logically: context → mechanisms → effectiveness → comparison → limitations → future
491	- Each question must be answerable from the evidence (don't ask about things not in the evidence)
492	- Assign each question an analytical_focus: aggregate, compare, explain, tabulate, or challenge
493	- Assign depth: 'deep' for the core questions (2-3), 'moderate' for supporting (3-4), 'brief' for peripheral (1-2)
494	- The set of questions should cover: what, how, how well, compared to what, and what's missing
495	- Generate a narrative_flow description explaining how the questions build on each other""".strip()
496	
497	
498	async def _decompose_into_questions(
499	    client: OpenRouterClient,
500	    query: str,
501	    evidence_summary: str,
502	    storm_perspectives: list[str],
503	) -> Optional[QuestionDecomposition]:
504	    """RC-3: Decompose research query into 6-10 sub-questions a reader would ask.
505	
506	    Returns None on failure so caller can fall back to cluster-based planning.
507	    """
508	    perspective_str = ", ".join(storm_perspectives[:8]) if storm_perspectives else "Scientific, Practical"
509	    prompt = (
510	        f"Research topic: {query}\n\n"
511	        f"Evidence available:\n{evidence_summary}\n\n"
512	        f"Perspectives represented: {perspective_str}\n\n"
513	        "Decompose this topic into 6-10 sub-questions that a knowledgeable reader "
514	        "would want answered. Each question should map to a report section."
515	    )
516	    try:
517	        result = await client.generate_structured(
518	            prompt=prompt,
519	            schema=QuestionDecomposition,
520	            system=_QUESTION_DECOMPOSITION_SYSTEM,
521	            max_tokens=PG_SYNTHESIS_STRUCTURED_MAX_TOKENS,
522	            timeout=int(os.getenv("PG_QUESTION_DECOMP_TIMEOUT", "300")),
523	        )
524	        if result and result.questions:
525	            logger.info(
526	                "[polaris graph] RC-3: Decomposed query into %d sub-questions",
527	                len(result.questions),
528	            )
529	            return result
530	        logger.warning("[polaris graph] RC-3: Decomposition returned 0 questions, falling back")
531	        return None
532	    except Exception as exc:
533	        logger.error(
534	            "[polaris graph] RC-3: Question decomposition failed: %s — falling back",
535	            str(exc)[:200],
536	        )
537	        return None

540	async def plan_report(
541	    client: OpenRouterClient,
542	    query: str,
543	    evidence: list[EvidencePiece],
544	    clusters: list[dict],
545	    quality_metrics: Optional[dict] = None,
546	    evidence_conflicts: Optional[list[dict]] = None,
547	    ltm_prior_knowledge: str = "",
548	) -> ReportOutline:
549	    """
550	    Plan the report structure using reasoning mode.
551	
552	    Returns a detailed outline with evidence assigned to sections.
553	
554	    FIX-QM27: Uses cluster-based outline prompt instead of listing all evidence.
555	    With 600+ evidence pieces, the original prompt overwhelmed the model (9K+ input
556	    tokens + 16K reasoning tokens = no room for JSON output). Now uses clusters
557	    (which already have evidence assignments) as the primary structure, with a
558	    compact evidence reference table.
559	
560	    FIX-ENV4: Accepts evidence_conflicts to inform outline structure. When
561	    conflicting evidence is detected, the outline prompt instructs the planner
562	    to dedicate sections or subsections to addressing these disagreements.
563	    """
564	    # RC-3: Question-driven planning (when enabled, build outline from sub-questions)
565	    if os.getenv("PG_V3_QUESTION_PLANNING", "0") == "1":
566	        from src.polaris_graph.state import STORM_PERSPECTIVES
567	        evidence_summary = _summarize_evidence_for_planning(clusters, evidence)
568	        decomposition = await _decompose_into_questions(
569	            client, query, evidence_summary,
570	            list(STORM_PERSPECTIVES) if hasattr(STORM_PERSPECTIVES, '__iter__') else [],
571	        )
572	        if decomposition and decomposition.questions:
573	            depth_to_words = {"deep": 1500, "moderate": 800, "brief": 400}
574	            sections = []
575	            for i, q in enumerate(decomposition.questions):
576	                sections.append(SectionOutlineItem(
577	                    section_id=f"s{i + 1:02d}",
578	                    title=q.question,
579	                    description=q.question,
580	                    analytical_focus=q.analytical_focus,
581	                    target_words=depth_to_words.get(q.expected_depth, 800),
582	                    evidence_ids=[],  # Filled by _assign_evidence_to_sections
583	                    order=i + 1,
584	                ))
585	            total_words = sum(s.target_words for s in sections)
586	            outline = ReportOutline(
587	                title=f"Research Report: {query}",
588	                sections=sections,
589	                total_target_words=total_words,
590	            )
591	            # Use existing evidence assignment machinery
592	            outline = _assign_evidence_to_sections(outline, clusters, evidence)
593	            outline = _validate_outline_evidence(outline)
594	            # Trim empty sections (keep at least 3)
595	            if len(outline.sections) > 3:
596	                non_empty = [s for s in outline.sections if s.evidence_ids]
597	                if non_empty and len(non_empty) >= 3:
598	                    outline.sections = non_empty
599	                    for idx, s in enumerate(outline.sections):
600	                        s.order = idx + 1
601	                    outline.total_target_words = sum(s.target_words for s in outline.sections)
602	            logger.info(
603	                "[polaris graph] RC-3: Question-driven outline: %d sections, %d words",
604	                len(outline.sections), outline.total_target_words,
605	            )
606	            return outline
607	        logger.warning("[polaris graph] RC-3: Question decomposition failed, falling back to cluster planning")
608	
609	    cluster_detail = _format_cluster_summary(clusters)
610	
611	    # FIX-303C: Build perspective list for diversity gate
612	    perspective_set: set[str] = set()
613	    for e in evidence:
614	        p = e.get("perspective")
615	        if p:
616	            perspective_set.add(p)
617	    perspective_list = ", ".join(sorted(perspective_set)) if perspective_set else "Scientific"
618	
619	    gold_count = sum(1 for e in evidence if e.get('quality_tier') == 'GOLD')
620	    silver_count = sum(1 for e in evidence if e.get('quality_tier') == 'SILVER')
621	
622	    # FIX-ENV4: Build conflict awareness block for outline prompt
623	    conflict_block = ""
624	    if evidence_conflicts:
625	        conflict_lines = []
626	        for i, conflict in enumerate(evidence_conflicts[:10], 1):
627	            conflict_lines.append(
628	                f"  {i}. [{conflict.get('evidence_a_id', '?')}] vs "
629	                f"[{conflict.get('evidence_b_id', '?')}] "
630	                f"(overlap={conflict.get('topic_overlap', 0):.0%}, "
631	                f"signals: {', '.join(conflict.get('contradiction_signals', [])[:3])})"
632	            )
633	        conflict_block = (
634	            f"\n\nEVIDENCE CONFLICTS DETECTED ({len(evidence_conflicts)} pairs):\n"
635	            + "\n".join(conflict_lines)
636	            + "\n\nIMPORTANT: When designing the outline, ensure that sections covering "
637	            "conflicting evidence explicitly address these disagreements. Dedicate "
638	            "paragraphs or subsections to comparing conflicting findings rather than "
639	            "presenting only one side."
640	        )
641	
642	    # M-15: Build LTM prior knowledge block
643	    ltm_block = ""
644	    if ltm_prior_knowledge:
645	        ltm_block = (
646	            f"\n\nPRIOR KNOWLEDGE (from previous research runs):\n"
647	            f"{ltm_prior_knowledge}\n\n"
648	            "Consider this prior knowledge when designing the outline. "
649	            "It may provide additional context or highlight aspects to cover."
650	        )
651	
652	    # FIX-056: Cap sections based on evidence count to prevent empty sections.
653	    # BUG-091: 12-section outline from 5 evidence pieces → 7 sections with 0 evidence.
654	    # FIX-057: Tighten cap for critically low evidence (<10) — 1 section per piece.
655	    # T052 root cause: 13-section outline from 3 evidence → 10 sections with 0 evidence.
656	    max_sections_cap = int(os.getenv("PG_MAX_OUTLINE_SECTIONS", "15"))
657	    if len(evidence) < 10:
658	        # Critical evidence starvation: 1 section per evidence piece, minimum 3
659	        target_sections = max(3, len(evidence))
660	    else:
661	        # Ensure at least 5 evidence per section to prevent thin sections.
662	        # 55 evidence / 5 = 11 sections max (not 15).
663	        _min_ev_per_section = int(os.getenv("PG_MIN_EVIDENCE_PER_SECTION", "5"))
664	        evidence_based_cap = min(max_sections_cap, len(evidence) // _min_ev_per_section)
665	        target_sections = max(3, evidence_based_cap)
666	    target_words = int(os.getenv('PG_TARGET_TOTAL_WORDS', '8000'))
667	
668	    prompt = f"""Research question: {query}
669	
670	Evidence collected: {len(evidence)} pieces (GOLD={gold_count}, SILVER={silver_count})
671	Perspectives: {perspective_list}
672	
673	{cluster_detail}
674	{conflict_block}
675	{ltm_block}
676	
677	Design a data-driven report outline where section depth matches evidence depth:
678	1. Covers ALL aspects of the research question
679	2. Has {target_sections} sections. Each section's target_words = min(200 + cluster_evidence_count * 80, 2000).
680	3. Flows logically from background to findings to implications
681	4. Each section should correspond to one or more clusters — state which cluster numbers it covers
682	5. Return evidence_ids as empty lists [] — evidence assignment is automated
683	6. Ensure perspective diversity: sections should draw from multiple perspectives
684	7. The abstract MUST NOT mention specific counts of sources or evidence
685	8. If a cluster has fewer than 3 evidence pieces, merge it into an "Additional Findings" section
686	9. Note which sections should include TABLES (clusters with 4+ comparable data points)"""
687	
688	    # FIX-QM12: Use PG_SYNTHESIS_STRUCTURED_MAX_TOKENS (16384) instead of
689	    # PG_SECTION_WRITER_MAX_TOKENS (8192). Reasoning consumes ~6000 tokens,
690	    # leaving only ~2000 for JSON at 8192, which truncates ReportOutline.
691	    outline = None
692	    try:
693	        outline = await client.generate_structured(
694	            prompt=prompt,
695	            schema=ReportOutline,
696	            system=OUTLINE_SYSTEM_PROMPT,
697	            max_tokens=PG_SYNTHESIS_STRUCTURED_MAX_TOKENS,
698	            timeout=int(os.getenv("PG_OUTLINE_TIMEOUT", "600")),
699	        )
700	
701	        # FIX-311: Retry once if outline has 0 sections (Kimi garbage output)
702	        if len(outline.sections) == 0:
703	            logger.warning(
704	                "[polaris graph] FIX-311: Outline returned 0 sections, retrying..."
705	            )
706	            outline = await client.generate_structured(
707	                prompt=prompt,
708	                schema=ReportOutline,
709	                system=OUTLINE_SYSTEM_PROMPT,
710	                max_tokens=PG_SYNTHESIS_STRUCTURED_MAX_TOKENS,
711	                timeout=600,
712	            )
713	    except Exception as outline_exc:
714	        logger.error(
715	            "[polaris graph] FIX-OUTLINE: Outline generation failed: %s "
716	            "— using fallback outline",
717	            str(outline_exc)[:200],
718	        )
719	        outline = None
720	
721	    # FIX-311: Fallback — build outline from clusters if LLM fails or returns 0
722	    if outline is None or len(outline.sections) == 0:
723	        logger.warning(
724	            "[polaris graph] FIX-311: Using fallback outline from %d clusters",
725	            len(clusters),
726	        )
727	        outline = _fallback_outline(query, clusters, evidence)
728	
729	    # FIX-OUTLINE: Algorithmically assign evidence from clusters to sections
730	    outline = _assign_evidence_to_sections(outline, clusters, evidence)
731	
732	    # FIX-A4: Validate and deduplicate evidence assignments across sections
733	    outline = _validate_outline_evidence(outline)
734	
735	    # FIX-056: Trim sections with 0 evidence (BUG-091).
736	    # LLM may generate more sections than evidence can support.
737	    # Keep at least 3 sections (intro/body/conclusion structure).
738	    pre_trim = len(outline.sections)
739	    if pre_trim > 3:
740	        non_empty = [s for s in outline.sections if s.evidence_ids]
741	        empty = [s for s in outline.sections if not s.evidence_ids]
742	        if non_empty and empty:
743	            outline.sections = non_empty
744	            # Re-number
745	            for i, s in enumerate(outline.sections):
746	                s.order = i + 1
747	            outline.total_target_words = len(outline.sections) * 800
748	            logger.info(
749	                "[polaris graph] FIX-056: Trimmed %d empty sections "
750	                "(%d → %d sections with evidence)",
751	                len(empty), pre_trim, len(outline.sections),
752	            )
753	
754	    logger.info(
755	        "[polaris graph] Report outline: %d sections, %d target words, '%s'",
756	        len(outline.sections),
757	        outline.total_target_words,
758	        outline.title,
759	    )
760	
761	    return outline

764	def _assign_evidence_to_sections(
765	    outline: ReportOutline,
766	    clusters: list[dict],
767	    evidence: list[EvidencePiece],
768	) -> ReportOutline:
769	    """FIX-OUTLINE: Algorithmically assign evidence from clusters to outline sections.
770	
771	    Uses word overlap (Jaccard similarity) between section title+description
772	    and cluster theme+description to find the best cluster->section mapping.
773	    Each cluster's evidence_ids are assigned to its best-matching section.
774	
775	    This replaces LLM-based evidence assignment, which was the root cause of
776	    outline timeouts (forcing the LLM to assign 900+ evidence IDs to sections
777	    within a structured JSON schema call).
778	    """
779	    if not clusters or not outline.sections:
780	        return outline
781	
782	    sorted_sections = sorted(outline.sections, key=lambda s: s.order)
783	
784	    def _word_set(text: str) -> set[str]:
785	        """Extract meaningful words (>2 chars) as a lowercase set."""
786	        return {w.lower() for w in text.split() if len(w) > 2}
787	
788	    # Build cluster word sets from theme + description
789	    cluster_word_sets = [
790	        _word_set(f"{c.get('theme', '')} {c.get('description', '')}")
791	        for c in clusters
792	    ]
793	
794	    # Build section word sets from title + description
795	    section_word_sets = [
796	        _word_set(f"{s.title} {s.description}")
797	        for s in sorted_sections
798	    ]
799	
800	    # Assign each cluster to its best-matching section
801	    section_evidence: dict[int, list[str]] = {
802	        i: [] for i in range(len(sorted_sections))
803	    }
804	    assigned_evidence_ids: set[str] = set()
805	
806	    for ci, cluster in enumerate(clusters):
807	        c_words = cluster_word_sets[ci]
808	        best_section_idx = 0
809	        best_similarity = -1.0
810	
811	        for si, s_words in enumerate(section_word_sets):
812	            if not c_words or not s_words:
813	                continue
814	            intersection = len(c_words & s_words)
815	            union = len(c_words | s_words)
816	            similarity = intersection / union if union > 0 else 0.0
817	            if similarity > best_similarity:
818	                best_similarity = similarity
819	                best_section_idx = si
820	
821	        ev_ids = cluster.get("evidence_ids", [])
822	        section_evidence[best_section_idx].extend(ev_ids)
823	        assigned_evidence_ids.update(ev_ids)
824	
825	    # FIX-MP6: Assign unassigned evidence to section with lowest count + any overlap.
826	    # This distributes evidence more evenly (prevents piling on large sections).
827	    all_evidence_ids = {
828	        e.get("evidence_id", "") for e in evidence if e.get("evidence_id")
829	    }
830	    unassigned = all_evidence_ids - assigned_evidence_ids
831	
832	    if unassigned:
833	        ev_map = {e.get("evidence_id", ""): e for e in evidence}
834	        for eid in unassigned:
835	            ev = ev_map.get(eid)
836	            if not ev:
837	                continue
838	            stmt_words = _word_set(ev.get("statement", ""))
839	            if not stmt_words:
840	                # No words to match — assign to section with fewest evidence
841	                min_idx = min(section_evidence, key=lambda k: len(section_evidence[k]))
842	                section_evidence[min_idx].append(eid)
843	                continue
844	
845	            # Find all sections with ANY word overlap, pick the one with fewest evidence
846	            candidates: list[tuple[int, int]] = []  # (section_idx, count)
847	            for si, s_words in enumerate(section_word_sets):
848	                if s_words and (stmt_words & s_words):
849	                    candidates.append((si, len(section_evidence[si])))
850	
851	            if candidates:
852	                # Pick section with lowest evidence count (ties: first match)
853	                best_idx = min(candidates, key=lambda t: t[1])[0]
854	            else:
855	                # No overlap at all — fall back to section with fewest evidence
856	                best_idx = min(section_evidence, key=lambda k: len(section_evidence[k]))
857	
858	            section_evidence[best_idx].append(eid)
859	
860	        logger.info(
861	            "[polaris graph] FIX-MP6: Redistributed %d unassigned evidence pieces "
862	            "to lowest-count sections with word overlap",
863	            len(unassigned),
864	        )
865	
866	    # FIX-CITE-DIV-1: Cap any single source at max_source_pct of section evidence
867	    max_source_pct = float(os.getenv("PG_MAX_SOURCE_PCT_PER_SECTION", "0.33"))
868	    ev_map_for_div = {e.get("evidence_id", ""): e for e in evidence}
869	    diversity_trimmed = 0
870	    for si in section_evidence:
871	        ids = section_evidence[si]
872	        if len(ids) < 6:
873	            continue  # Too few to worry about diversity
874	        # Count evidence per source URL
875	        source_counts: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
876	        for eid in ids:
877	            ev = ev_map_for_div.get(eid, {})
878	            src_url = ev.get("source_url", "unknown")
879	            source_counts.setdefault(src_url, []).append(eid)
880	        max_per_source = max(3, int(len(ids) * max_source_pct))
881	        trimmed_ids = []
882	        for src_url, src_ids in source_counts.items():
883	            if len(src_ids) > max_per_source:
884	                # Keep highest-relevance evidence from this source
885	                src_ids_sorted = sorted(
886	                    src_ids,
887	                    key=lambda eid: ev_map_for_div.get(eid, {}).get("relevance_score", 0),
888	                    reverse=True,
889	                )
890	                trimmed_ids.extend(src_ids_sorted[:max_per_source])
891	                diversity_trimmed += len(src_ids) - max_per_source
892	            else:
893	                trimmed_ids.extend(src_ids)
894	        section_evidence[si] = trimmed_ids
895	
896	    if diversity_trimmed > 0:
897	        logger.info(
898	            "[polaris graph] FIX-CITE-DIV-1: Trimmed %d over-represented "
899	            "source evidence across sections (max %.0f%% per source)",
900	            diversity_trimmed, max_source_pct * 100,
901	        )
902	
903	    # FIX-RISK-QUORUM: if a section's title or description contains risk-axis
904	    # terms, ensure it receives at least PG_RISK_QUORUM_MIN risk-axis evidence
905	    # pieces (pulled from the full pool, bypassing cluster routing). Without
906	    # this, a Jaccard word-overlap cluster-to-section map can starve a risk
907	    # section when clusters were themed around benefits.
908	    risk_axis_section_terms = (
909	        "risk", "adverse", "safety", "harm", "side effect",
910	        "side-effect", "contraindicat",
911	    )
912	    risk_axis_ev_categories = {
913	        "risk", "adverse_event", "contraindication", "safety",
914	    }
915	    risk_axis_ev_keywords = (
916	        "adverse", "contraindicat", "side effect", "side-effect",
917	        "hypoglyc", "disorder", "eating disorder", "bone density",
918	        "muscle loss", "lean body mass", "pregnancy", "pregnant",
919	        "adolescent", "teen", "mortality", "harm",
920	        "dizziness", "nausea", "irritab",
921	    )
922	    quorum_min = int(os.getenv("PG_RISK_QUORUM_MIN", "2"))
923	    quorum_added = 0
924	    for si, section in enumerate(sorted_sections):
925	        title_desc = f"{section.title} {section.description}".lower()
926	        if not any(t in title_desc for t in risk_axis_section_terms):
927	            continue
928	        existing_ids = set(section_evidence[si])
929	        existing_risk_count = 0
930	        for eid in existing_ids:
931	            ev = ev_map_for_div.get(eid, {})
932	            cat = (ev.get("fact_category", "") or "").lower()
933	            blob = (
934	                (ev.get("statement", "") or "")
935	                + " "
936	                + (ev.get("direct_quote", "") or "")
937	            ).lower()
938	            if cat in risk_axis_ev_categories or any(
939	                k in blob for k in risk_axis_ev_keywords
940	            ):
941	                existing_risk_count += 1
942	        needed = max(0, quorum_min - existing_risk_count)
943	        if needed == 0:
944	            continue
945	        # Find candidate risk-axis evidence not yet in this section.
946	        candidates = []
947	        for ev in evidence:
948	            eid = ev.get("evidence_id", "")
949	            if not eid or eid in existing_ids:
950	                continue
951	            cat = (ev.get("fact_category", "") or "").lower()
952	            blob = (
953	                (ev.get("statement", "") or "")
954	                + " "
955	                + (ev.get("direct_quote", "") or "")
956	            ).lower()
957	            is_risk = (
958	                cat in risk_axis_ev_categories
959	                or any(k in blob for k in risk_axis_ev_keywords)
960	                or ev.get("risk_axis_retained") is True
961	            )
962	            if is_risk:
963	                candidates.append(
964	                    (eid, ev.get("relevance_score", 0.0))
965	                )
966	        # Prefer higher relevance, break ties by appearance order.
967	        candidates.sort(key=lambda t: t[1], reverse=True)
968	        for eid, _ in candidates[:needed]:
969	            section_evidence[si].append(eid)
970	            quorum_added += 1
971	
972	    if quorum_added > 0:
973	        logger.info(
974	            "[polaris graph] FIX-RISK-QUORUM: Injected %d risk-axis evidence "
975	            "pieces into risk/safety-titled sections (min=%d per section)",
976	            quorum_added, quorum_min,
977	        )
978	
979	    # Populate outline sections with assigned evidence
980	    for si, section in enumerate(sorted_sections):
981	        section.evidence_ids = section_evidence.get(si, [])
982	
983	    total_assigned = sum(len(ids) for ids in section_evidence.values())
984	    logger.info(
985	        "[polaris graph] FIX-OUTLINE: Algorithmically assigned %d evidence pieces "
986	        "to %d sections from %d clusters",
987	        total_assigned,
988	        len(outline.sections),
989	        len(clusters),
990	    )
991	
992	    return outline

995	def _validate_outline_evidence(outline: ReportOutline) -> ReportOutline:
996	    """Deduplicate evidence assignments and ensure minimum per section.
997	
998	    1. Checks for evidence_ids appearing in more than one section.
999	       For any duplicates, keeps the evidence in the section where it
1000	       appears first (by section order) and removes from later sections.
1001	    2. FIX-QG1: Ensures every section has >= 3 evidence_ids by
1002	       redistributing from over-assigned sections.
1003	    """
1004	    min_evidence_per_section = int(
1005	        os.getenv("PG_MIN_EVIDENCE_PER_SECTION", "3")
1006	    )
1007	
1008	    seen: set[str] = set()
1009	    dedup_count = 0
1010	
1011	    sorted_sections = sorted(outline.sections, key=lambda s: s.order)
1012	    for section in sorted_sections:
1013	        unique_ids = []
1014	        for eid in section.evidence_ids:
1015	            if eid in seen:
1016	                dedup_count += 1
1017	            else:
1018	                seen.add(eid)
1019	                unique_ids.append(eid)
1020	        section.evidence_ids = unique_ids
1021	
1022	    if dedup_count > 0:
1023	        logger.warning(
1024	            "[polaris graph] FIX-C1: Deduplicated %d cross-section evidence assignments",
1025	            dedup_count,
1026	        )
1027	
1028	    # FIX-QG1+STARVATION: Redistribute evidence to starved sections.
1029	    # Old: donor threshold >10 evidence. With 53 evidence / 8 sections = 6.6 avg,
1030	    # NO section had >10 → 3 sections got 0 evidence → empty sections.
1031	    # New: adaptive donor threshold = max(avg+2, min_evidence_per_section+1).
1032	    # Donors keep at least min_evidence_per_section, donate the rest.
1033	    starved = [s for s in sorted_sections if len(s.evidence_ids) < min_evidence_per_section]
1034	    if starved:
1035	        total_ev = sum(len(s.evidence_ids) for s in sorted_sections)
1036	        avg_ev = total_ev / max(len(sorted_sections), 1)
1037	        donor_threshold = max(int(avg_ev) + 2, min_evidence_per_section + 1)
1038	        keep_minimum = min_evidence_per_section  # Donors keep at least this many
1039	
1040	        donor_pool: list[str] = []
1041	        for section in sorted_sections:
1042	            if len(section.evidence_ids) > donor_threshold:
1043	                excess = section.evidence_ids[keep_minimum:]
1044	                section.evidence_ids = section.evidence_ids[:keep_minimum]
1045	                donor_pool.extend(excess)
1046	
1047	        # Distribute from donor pool to starved sections
1048	        redistributed = 0
1049	        for section in starved:
1050	            needed = min_evidence_per_section - len(section.evidence_ids)
1051	            while needed > 0 and donor_pool:
1052	                section.evidence_ids.append(donor_pool.pop(0))
1053	                needed -= 1
1054	                redistributed += 1
1055	
1056	        if redistributed > 0:
1057	            logger.info(
1058	                "[polaris graph] FIX-QG1: Redistributed %d evidence_ids to "
1059	                "%d starved sections (min=%d per section)",
1060	                redistributed,
1061	                len(starved),
1062	                min_evidence_per_section,
1063	            )
1064	
1065	        # Log remaining starved sections (couldn't fix)
1066	        still_starved = [
1067	            s for s in sorted_sections
1068	            if len(s.evidence_ids) < min_evidence_per_section
1069	        ]
1070	        if still_starved:
1071	            logger.warning(
1072	                "[polaris graph] FIX-QG1: %d sections still below %d evidence_ids "
1073	                "after redistribution: %s",
1074	                len(still_starved),
1075	                min_evidence_per_section,
1076	                [s.title[:40] for s in still_starved],
1077	            )
1078	
1079	    return outline

1082	def _fallback_outline(
1083	    query: str,
1084	    clusters: list[dict],
1085	    evidence: list[EvidencePiece],
1086	) -> ReportOutline:
1087	    """Build a default outline from evidence clusters when LLM fails."""
1088	    sections = []
1089	    for i, cluster in enumerate(clusters):
1090	        # FIX-T5: Use "theme" key (from map-reduce clusters), fall back to "label"
1091	        cluster_label = cluster.get("theme", cluster.get("label", f"Theme {i + 1}"))
1092	        # FIX-T5: Use evidence_ids directly from cluster if available
1093	        evidence_ids = cluster.get("evidence_ids", [])
1094	        # Fallback: match by cluster_id in evidence
1095	        if not evidence_ids:
1096	            evidence_ids = [
1097	                e.get("evidence_id", "") for e in evidence
1098	                if e.get("cluster_id") == cluster.get("cluster_id")
1099	            ]
1100	        # Also try matching by string cluster_id
1101	        if not evidence_ids:
1102	            evidence_ids = [
1103	                e.get("evidence_id", "") for e in evidence
1104	                if str(e.get("cluster_id", "")) == str(cluster.get("cluster_id", ""))
1105	            ]
1106	        # If still empty, assign first N/clusters evidence pieces
1107	        if not evidence_ids and evidence:
1108	            chunk_size = max(1, len(evidence) // max(len(clusters), 1))
1109	            start = i * chunk_size
1110	            evidence_ids = [
1111	                e.get("evidence_id", "")
1112	                for e in evidence[start:start + chunk_size]
1113	            ]
1114	        # FIX-MP9: Build descriptive title — detect generic single-word themes
1115	        # and construct title from top evidence keywords instead.
1116	        _GENERIC_THEMES = {
1117	            "statistic", "finding", "citation", "descriptive", "overview",
1118	            "analysis", "discussion", "conclusion", "results", "methodology",
1119	            "reference", "data", "evidence", "information", "summary",
1120	            "theme", "topic", "section", "category", "cluster",
1121	        }
1122	        desc = cluster.get("description", "")
1123	        # FIX-CITE-3/R2: Strip "Evidence categorized as X" from description —
1124	        # this is the category fallback pattern and produces garbage titles.
1125	        desc = re.sub(r"^Evidence categorized as \w+\s*", "", desc).strip()
1126	        theme_words = cluster_label.strip().split()
1127	        is_generic = (
1128	            len(theme_words) == 1
1129	            and theme_words[0].lower().rstrip("s") in _GENERIC_THEMES
1130	        )
1131	
1132	        if is_generic and evidence_ids:
1133	            # Extract top keywords from evidence statements for this cluster.
1134	            # FIX-CITE-3/R2: Use keywords as the FULL title (no desc suffix).
1135	            from collections import Counter
1136	            _stopwords = {
1137	                "the", "and", "for", "that", "this", "with", "from", "are",
1138	                "was", "were", "has", "have", "been", "its", "can", "may",
1139	                "not", "but", "also", "more", "than", "into", "such", "each",
1140	                "will", "about", "which", "their", "other", "these", "some",
1141	            }
1142	            word_counts: Counter = Counter()
1143	            ev_map_local = {e.get("evidence_id", ""): e for e in evidence}
1144	            for eid in evidence_ids:
1145	                ev_item = ev_map_local.get(eid)
1146	                if ev_item:
1147	                    for w in ev_item.get("statement", "").split():
1148	                        wl = w.lower().strip(".,;:()[]\"'")
1149	                        if len(wl) > 3 and wl not in _stopwords:
1150	                            word_counts[wl] += 1
1151	            top_keywords = [w for w, _ in word_counts.most_common(5)]
1152	            if len(top_keywords) >= 3:
1153	                fallback_title = " ".join(top_keywords[:3]).title()
1154	                # FIX-CITE-3/R2: Only append desc if it adds value (non-empty after stripping)
1155	                if desc and len(desc) > 10:
1156	                    fallback_title = f"{fallback_title}: {desc[:50].rstrip('.,; ')}"
1157	            elif desc and len(desc) > 10:
1158	                fallback_title = desc[:80].rstrip(".,; ")
1159	            else:
1160	                fallback_title = f"Analysis of {cluster_label}"
1161	        elif desc and len(desc) > 10 and len(theme_words) <= 2:
1162	            fallback_title = f"{cluster_label}: {desc[:60].rstrip('.,; ')}"
1163	        else:
1164	            fallback_title = cluster_label[:100]
1165	        sections.append(SectionOutlineItem(
1166	            section_id=f"s{i + 1:02d}",
1167	            title=fallback_title,
1168	            description=cluster.get("description", f"Analysis of {cluster_label}"),
1169	            evidence_ids=[eid for eid in evidence_ids if eid],
1170	            target_words=800,
1171	            order=i + 1,
1172	        ))
1173	
1174	    title = f"Research Analysis: {query[:80]}"
1175	    abstract = (
1176	        f"This comprehensive research report directly addresses the question: {query[:200]}. "
1177	        f"Drawing on {len(evidence)} evidence pieces from {len(clusters)} thematic areas, "
1178	        f"this analysis examines the key findings, current evidence, and implications "
1179	        f"relevant to this research question."
1180	    )
1181	    return ReportOutline(
1182	        title=title,
1183	        abstract=abstract,
1184	        sections=sections,
1185	        total_target_words=len(sections) * 800,
1186	    )

2412	                    if e.get("source_url", "") not in over_cited_urls
2413	                ]
2414	                deprioritized = [
2415	                    e for e in filtered_ev
2416	                    if e.get("source_url", "") in over_cited_urls
2417	                ]
2418	                section_filtered_evidence[sid] = primary + deprioritized
2419	            logger.info(
2420	                "[polaris graph] FIX-9: %d sources over-cited (>=%d), "
2421	                "deprioritized for remaining sections",
2422	                len(over_cited_urls),
2423	                max_citation_freq,
2424	            )
2425	
2426	        # NRC-2 + FIX-CITE-3/C1: Extract key claims AND specific statistics
2427	        # from newly written sections to prevent cross-section repetition.
2428	        # Focus on concrete data points (numbers with units) which the LLM
2429	        # is most likely to repeat verbatim across sections.
2430	        for draft in batch_results:
2431	            if isinstance(draft, Exception):
2432	                continue
2433	            # Extract sentences with citations as "covered claims"
2434	            sentences = re.split(r'(?<=[.!?])\s+(?=[A-Z])', draft.content)
2435	            for sent in sentences:
2436	                if "[CITE:" in sent and len(sent.split()) >= 8:
2437	                    # Truncate to key claim (first 120 chars)
2438	                    claim = sent.strip()[:120]
2439	                    if claim not in all_covered_claims:
2440	                        all_covered_claims.append(claim)
2441	
2442	            # FIX-CITE-3/C1: Extract specific statistics (numbers + units/context)
2443	            # These are the data points most likely to be repeated verbatim.
2444	            stats = re.findall(
2445	                r'(\d+[\.\d]*\s*(?:kg|%|mmol|mg|pmol|mmHg|hours?|weeks?|months?'
2446	                r'|years?|participants?|studies|trials?|adults?))',
2447	                draft.content, re.IGNORECASE,
2448	            )
2449	            for stat in stats:
2450	                stat_claim = f"STATISTIC: {stat.strip()}"
2451	                if stat_claim not in all_covered_claims:
2452	                    all_covered_claims.append(stat_claim)
2453	
2454	            # Phase 3B: Cap covered claims to last 20 statistics + recent claims
2455	            _max_covered_stats = int(os.getenv("PG_MAX_COVERED_STATS", "20"))
2456	            _max_covered_claims = int(os.getenv("PG_MAX_COVERED_CLAIMS", "50"))
2457	            stats_in_claims = [c for c in all_covered_claims if c.startswith("STATISTIC:")]
2458	            other_claims = [c for c in all_covered_claims if not c.startswith("STATISTIC:")]
2459	            if len(stats_in_claims) > _max_covered_stats:
2460	                stats_in_claims = stats_in_claims[-_max_covered_stats:]
2461	            if len(other_claims) > _max_covered_claims:
2462	                other_claims = other_claims[-_max_covered_claims:]
2463	            all_covered_claims[:] = other_claims + stats_in_claims
2464	
2465	        # Phase 3B: Generate section summaries for cross-referencing
2466	        # Each section gets a 1-sentence summary of its key finding
2467	        _max_prev_summaries = int(os.getenv("PG_MAX_PREV_SUMMARIES", "5"))
2468	        if drafts:
2469	            prev_summaries = []
2470	            for d in drafts[-_max_prev_summaries:]:
2471	                # Extract first cited sentence as summary (more informative than truncation)
2472	                _sentences = re.split(r'(?<=[.!?])\s+(?=[A-Z])', getattr(d, "content", ""))
2473	                _summary_sent = ""
2474	                for _s in _sentences:
2475	                    if "[CITE:" in _s and len(_s.split()) >= 8:
2476	                        _summary_sent = _s.strip()[:150]
2477	                        break
2478	                if not _summary_sent:
2479	                    _summary_sent = getattr(d, "content", "")[:150].rsplit(" ", 1)[0] + "..."
2480	                prev_summaries.append(f"- '{d.title}': {_summary_sent}")
2481	            combined = "\n".join(prev_summaries)
2482	            if len(combined) > 2000:
2483	                combined = combined[:2000] + "\n..."
2484	            prev_summary = f"Previously written sections (do NOT repeat their facts or claims):\n{combined}"
2485	
2486	    # SF-33: Minimum sections gate — warn if too few sections were written
2487	    if len(sorted_sections) > 0 and len(drafts) < len(sorted_sections) * 0.6:
2488	        logger.error(
2489	            "[polaris graph] Only %d/%d sections written (%.0f%%) — "
2490	            "insufficient for a complete report",
2491	            len(drafts),
2492	            len(sorted_sections),
2493	            (len(drafts) / len(sorted_sections)) * 100,
2494	        )
2495	
2496	    logger.info(
2497	        "[polaris graph] Wrote %d/%d sections",
2498	        len(drafts),
2499	        len(sorted_sections),
2500	    )
2501	
2502	    return drafts, section_evidence_map

2505	async def expand_thin_sections(
2506	    client: OpenRouterClient,
2507	    thin_sections: list[ReportSection],
2508	    outline: ReportOutline,
2509	    evidence: list[EvidencePiece],
2510	    query: str,
2511	    target_expansion: int = 300,
2512	) -> list[SectionDraft]:
2513	    """
2514	    FIX-310: Expand thin sections by adding content from unused evidence.
2515	
2516	    For each thin section, builds a prompt with existing content + evidence
2517	    assigned to that section, asking the LLM to expand with ~300 additional words.
2518	
2519	    This is NOT a re-synthesis. Content is only added, never removed.
2520	    Returns updated SectionDraft objects for the expanded sections.
2521	    """
2522	    # Build evidence lookup for efficient filtering
2523	    evidence_map = {e.get("evidence_id", ""): e for e in evidence}
2524	
2525	    # Find which evidence IDs are already used across the report
2526	    used_evidence_ids: set[str] = set()
2527	    for s in thin_sections:
2528	        used_evidence_ids.update(s.get("evidence_ids", []))
2529	
2530	    expanded_drafts: list[SectionDraft] = []
2531	
2532	    for section in thin_sections:
2533	        section_id = section["section_id"]
2534	        existing_content = section["content"]
2535	        existing_words = section["word_count"]
2536	
2537	        # Find evidence assigned to this section from the outline
2538	        outline_section = None
2539	        for os_item in outline.sections:
2540	            if os_item.section_id == section_id:
2541	                outline_section = os_item
2542	                break
2543	
2544	        if not outline_section:
2545	            continue
2546	
2547	        # Get evidence for this section, prioritizing unused pieces
2548	        section_evidence = [
2549	            evidence_map[eid]
2550	            for eid in outline_section.evidence_ids
2551	            if eid in evidence_map
2552	        ]
2553	
2554	        # Also find unused evidence from the same cluster/category
2555	        unused_evidence = [
2556	            e for e in evidence
2557	            if e.get("evidence_id") not in used_evidence_ids
2558	            and e.get("relevance_score", 0) >= 0.3
2559	        ]
2560	        # Add top unused evidence (sorted by relevance)
2561	        unused_evidence.sort(key=lambda e: e.get("relevance_score", 0), reverse=True)
2562	        additional = unused_evidence[:10]  # Cap at 10 extra pieces
2563	
2564	        all_section_evidence = section_evidence + additional
2565	        # FIX-C6: Guard against empty evidence in expansion
2566	        if not all_section_evidence:
2567	            logger.warning(
2568	                "[polaris graph] FIX-C6: Section '%s' has no evidence for expansion, skipping",
2569	                section.get("title", "?"),
2570	            )
2571	            continue
2572	        evidence_text = _format_evidence_for_writing(all_section_evidence)
2573	
2574	        prompt = f"""You are expanding an existing report section with additional detail.
2575	
2576	Report section title: {section["title"]}
2577	Research question: {query}
2578	
2579	EXISTING CONTENT (DO NOT MODIFY OR REMOVE ANY OF THIS):
2580	---
2581	{existing_content}
2582	---
2583	
2584	Additional evidence available for expansion:
2585	{evidence_text}
2586	
2587	TASK: Write {target_expansion} additional words to ADD to the end of the existing content above.
2588	Do NOT repeat or rewrite any existing content. Only write NEW paragraphs that expand on the topic.
2589	Every factual claim MUST include a [CITE:evidence_id] marker.
2590	Use transition phrases to connect smoothly to the existing content.
2591	Write in third person, academic register."""
2592	
2593	        _exp_n_evidence = len(all_section_evidence)
2594	        _exp_suggested_words = min(200 + _exp_n_evidence * 80, 2000)
2595	        system = SECTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT.format(
2596	            n_evidence=_exp_n_evidence,
2597	            suggested_words=_exp_suggested_words,
2598	        )
2599	
2600	        try:
2601	            response = await client.generate(
2602	                prompt=prompt,
2603	                system=system,
2604	                max_tokens=PG_SECTION_CONTINUATION_MAX_TOKENS,  # FIX-C5
2605	                temperature=0.7,
2606	                reasoning_exclude=True,  # S1 (W1.3)
2607	            )
2608	            new_content = response.content.strip()
2609	
2610	            if not new_content or len(new_content.split()) < 30:
2611	                logger.warning(
2612	                    "[polaris graph] FIX-310: Expansion too short for '%s' (%d words)",
2613	                    section["title"],
2614	                    len(new_content.split()) if new_content else 0,
2615	                )
2616	                continue
2617	
2618	            # Scrub any CoT leakage
2619	            new_content = _scrub_cot(new_content)
2620	            new_content = _clean_artifacts(new_content, section_titles=[section.get("title", "")])  # FIX-059-C + FIX-060-E
2621	            new_content = _limit_transitions(new_content)  # FIX-059-K
2622	            new_content = _break_long_paragraphs(new_content)  # FIX-059-L
2623	
2624	            # FIX-059-I (H-05): Lightweight hedging enforcement on expansion content.
2625	            # write_section() enforces hedging via LLM revision but expansion skips it.
2626	            # Apply regex-based reduction: replace weak hedges with definitive language
2627	            # when the sentence has a citation (evidence-backed = no need to hedge).
2628	            _hedge_in_cited = re.compile(
2629	                r'\b(may|might|could|potentially)\b(?=[^.]*\[CITE:)',
2630	                re.IGNORECASE,
2631	            )
2632	            hedge_replacements = {
2633	                "may": "", "might": "", "could": "can",
2634	                "potentially": "",
2635	            }
2636	            def _replace_hedge(m):
2637	                word = m.group(1).lower()
2638	                return hedge_replacements.get(word, m.group(1))
2639	            hedged_content = _hedge_in_cited.sub(_replace_hedge, new_content)
2640	            if hedged_content != new_content:
2641	                hedge_count = len(_hedge_in_cited.findall(new_content))
2642	                logger.debug(
2643	                    "[polaris graph] FIX-059-I: Removed %d hedging words from "
2644	                    "cited claims in expansion of '%s'",
2645	                    hedge_count, section["title"][:40],
2646	                )
2647	                new_content = hedged_content
2648	
2649	            # Guard: reject expansion output that contains prompt echoes,
2650	            # evidence requests, or LLM meta-instructions.
2651	            _poison_markers = [
2652	                "please provide", "I cannot", "evidence content",
2653	                "To proceed", "evidence pieces", "Without the",
2654	                "I need the", "provide the evidence",
2655	            ]
2656	            if any(m.lower() in new_content.lower() for m in _poison_markers):
2657	                logger.warning(
2658	                    "[polaris graph] EXPANSION-GUARD: Rejected poisoned expansion "
2659	                    "for '%s' (prompt echo detected)", section["title"][:40],
2660	                )
2661	                continue
2662	
2663	            # Combine existing + new content
2664	            combined = existing_content.rstrip() + "\n\n" + new_content
2665	
2666	            # Extract claims from new content
2667	            claims = [
2668	                line.strip()
2669	                for line in new_content.split(".")
2670	                if "[CITE:" in line and len(line.strip()) > 20
2671	            ]
2672	
2673	            # FIX-059-I (H-04): Parse new CITE markers from expansion content
2674	            # and merge into evidence_ids so downstream audit sees all cited evidence
2675	            existing_eids = set(section.get("evidence_ids", []))
2676	            new_cite_ids = set(re.findall(r'\[CITE:(ev_[a-f0-9]+)\]', new_content))
2677	            merged_eids = list(existing_eids | new_cite_ids)
2678	            if new_cite_ids - existing_eids:
2679	                logger.debug(
2680	                    "[polaris graph] FIX-059-I: Expansion added %d new evidence_ids "
2681	                    "to section '%s'",
2682	                    len(new_cite_ids - existing_eids),
2683	                    section["title"][:40],
2684	                )
2685	
2686	            expanded = SectionDraft(
2687	                section_id=section_id,
2688	                title=section["title"],
2689	                content=combined,
2690	                claims_made=claims[:50],
2691	                evidence_ids=merged_eids,  # FIX-059-I: Merged existing + new
2692	            )
2693	
2694	            new_word_count = len(combined.split())
2695	            logger.info(
2696	                "[polaris graph] FIX-310: Expanded '%s': %d -> %d words (+%d)",
2697	                section["title"],
2698	                existing_words,
2699	                new_word_count,
2700	                new_word_count - existing_words,
2701	            )
2702	
2703	            # OBS-6: Trace section expansion
2704	            tracer = get_tracer()
2705	            if tracer:
2706	                tracer.llm_call(
2707	                    "synthesize", "section_expand",
2708	                    section_id=section_id,
2709	                    original_words=existing_words,
2710	                    expanded_words=new_word_count,
2711	                    added_words=new_word_count - existing_words,
2712	                    title=section["title"],
2713	                    content=combined[:8000],
2714	                    model=client.model,
2715	                )
2716	
2717	            expanded_drafts.append(expanded)
2718	
2719	        except Exception as exc:
2720	            logger.error(
2721	                "[polaris graph] FIX-310: Failed to expand '%s': %s",
2722	                section["title"],
2723	                str(exc)[:200],
2724	            )
2725	
2726	    return expanded_drafts
