2729	async def revise_section(
2730	    client: OpenRouterClient,
2731	    draft: SectionDraft,
2732	    evidence: list[EvidencePiece],
2733	    query: str,
2734	    report_title: str,
2735	) -> SectionDraft:
2736	    """FIX-S1: Revise a section for quality: strengthen arguments, improve sourcing,
2737	    fix logical flow. Content can be rewritten but core findings must be preserved."""
2738	
2739	    # Extract cited evidence IDs from the draft content
2740	    cited_ids = set(re.findall(r"\[CITE:(ev_[a-f0-9]+)\]", draft.content))
2741	    section_evidence = [
2742	        e for e in evidence if e.get("evidence_id") in cited_ids
2743	    ]
2744	    # Also include nearby high-relevance evidence not yet cited
2745	    uncited_high = [
2746	        e for e in evidence
2747	        if e.get("evidence_id") not in cited_ids
2748	        and e.get("relevance_score", 0) >= 0.5
2749	        and e.get("quality_tier") in ("GOLD", "SILVER")
2750	    ][:10]
2751	    all_evidence = section_evidence + uncited_high
2752	
2753	    evidence_text = _format_evidence_for_writing(all_evidence)
2754	    draft_word_count = len(draft.content.split())
2755	
2756	    prompt = f"""You are a senior research editor revising a report section.
2757	
2758	Report title: {report_title}
2759	Research question: {query}
2760	Section title: {draft.title}
2761	
2762	CURRENT DRAFT:
2763	{draft.content}
2764	
2765	AVAILABLE EVIDENCE (you may cite additional pieces not yet used):
2766	{evidence_text}
2767	
2768	REVISION INSTRUCTIONS:
2769	1. Strengthen weak arguments -- every claim must have clear evidence support.
2770	2. Remove redundant or tangential sentences that dilute the section's focus.
2771	3. Ensure logical flow: each paragraph should build on the previous one.
2772	4. Use precise academic language. Replace vague hedging with specific claims.
2773	5. Ensure EVERY factual claim has a [CITE:evidence_id] marker.
2774	6. Preserve the section's core findings and conclusions.
2775	7. Target {draft_word_count} words (same length, better quality).
2776	8. Use transition words SPARINGLY — no more than 1 per 200 words. Vary transitions and do NOT start consecutive sentences with them.
2777	9. Do NOT include any chain-of-thought, reasoning, or planning text."""
2778	
2779	    _rev_n_evidence = len(all_evidence)
2780	    _rev_suggested_words = min(200 + _rev_n_evidence * 80, 2000)
2781	    system = SECTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT.format(
2782	        n_evidence=_rev_n_evidence,
2783	        suggested_words=_rev_suggested_words,
2784	    )
2785	
2786	    try:
2787	        response = await client.generate(
2788	            prompt=prompt,
2789	            system=system,
2790	            max_tokens=PG_SECTION_WRITER_MAX_TOKENS,
2791	            temperature=0.5,  # Lower temp for revision (more faithful to original)
2792	            reasoning_exclude=True,  # S1 (W1.3)
2793	        )
2794	        revised = response.content.strip()
2795	        revised = _scrub_cot(revised)
2796	
2797	        # Safety: only accept revision if it's at least 70% of original length
2798	        revised_words = len(revised.split())
2799	        if revised_words < draft_word_count * 0.7:
2800	            logger.warning(
2801	                "[polaris graph] FIX-S1: Revision of '%s' too short "
2802	                "(%.0f%% of original), keeping draft",
2803	                draft.title,
2804	                (revised_words / max(draft_word_count, 1)) * 100,
2805	            )
2806	            return draft
2807	
2808	        claims = [
2809	            line.strip()
2810	            for line in revised.split(".")
2811	            if "[CITE:" in line and len(line.strip()) > 20
2812	        ]
2813	
2814	        logger.info(
2815	            "[polaris graph] FIX-S1: Revised '%s': %d -> %d words, %d claims",
2816	            draft.title,
2817	            draft_word_count,
2818	            revised_words,
2819	            len(claims),
2820	        )
2821	
2822	        return SectionDraft(
2823	            section_id=draft.section_id,
2824	            title=draft.title,
2825	            content=revised,
2826	            claims_made=claims[:50],
2827	            evidence_ids=draft.evidence_ids,  # FIX-039: Preserve across revision
2828	        )
2829	
2830	    except Exception as exc:
2831	        logger.error(
2832	            "[polaris graph] FIX-S1: Revision failed for '%s': %s — keeping draft",
2833	            draft.title,
2834	            str(exc)[:200],
2835	        )
2836	        return draft

2840	# Formatting helpers

2843	def _format_evidence_summary(evidence: list[EvidencePiece]) -> str:
2844	    """Format evidence for the outliner (compact)."""
2845	    lines = []
2846	    for e in evidence[:200]:  # Cap at 200 for prompt length
2847	        quality = e.get("quality_tier", "?")
2848	        relevance = e.get("relevance_score", 0)
2849	        lines.append(
2850	            f"- [{e.get('evidence_id', '?')}] ({quality}, rel={relevance:.2f}) "
2851	            f"{e.get('statement', '')[:150]}"
2852	        )
2853	    return "\n".join(lines)
2854	
2855	
2856	def _format_cluster_detail(
2857	    clusters: list[dict], evidence: list[EvidencePiece]
2858	) -> str:
2859	    """FIX-QM27: Format clusters with evidence summaries for compact outline prompt.
2860	
2861	    Instead of listing all 600+ evidence pieces individually (~40K chars),
2862	    groups them by cluster with truncated statements (~5-8K chars total).
2863	    This keeps the outline prompt within the model's effective output budget.
2864	    """
2865	    # Build evidence lookup
2866	    ev_map: dict[str, EvidencePiece] = {
2867	        e.get("evidence_id", ""): e for e in evidence
2868	    }
2869	
2870	    parts = []
2871	    assigned_ids: set[str] = set()
2872	
2873	    # FIX-2: Cap per-cluster evidence display to keep outline prompt compact.
2874	    # With 15 clusters x 850 evidence, old limit of 40/cluster = 30K+ chars
2875	    # causing 3x timeout + fallback. Now configurable, default 10.
2876	    max_outline_ev = int(os.getenv("PG_OUTLINE_EVIDENCE_PER_CLUSTER", "10"))
2877	
2878	    for i, c in enumerate(clusters, 1):
2879	        theme = c.get("theme", "Unknown")
2880	        desc = c.get("description", "")[:200]
2881	        ev_ids = c.get("evidence_ids", [])
2882	        strength = c.get("strength", "moderate")
2883	
2884	        parts.append(
2885	            f"## Cluster {i}: {theme} ({len(ev_ids)} evidence, strength={strength})"
2886	        )
2887	        parts.append(f"Description: {desc}")
2888	
2889	        # FIX-2: Show only top evidence per cluster, prioritizing GOLD/SILVER
2890	        # Sort evidence IDs by quality tier (GOLD first, then SILVER, then BRONZE)
2891	        tier_order = {"GOLD": 0, "SILVER": 1, "BRONZE": 2}
2892	        sorted_ev_ids = sorted(
2893	            ev_ids,
2894	            key=lambda eid: tier_order.get(
2895	                ev_map.get(eid, {}).get("quality_tier", "BRONZE"), 2
2896	            ),
2897	        )
2898	
2899	        shown = 0
2900	        for eid in sorted_ev_ids:
2901	            if shown >= max_outline_ev:
2902	                break
2903	            ev = ev_map.get(eid)
2904	            if ev:
2905	                quality = ev.get("quality_tier", "?")
2906	                stmt = ev.get("statement", "")[:120]
2907	                parts.append(f"  - [{eid}] ({quality}) {stmt}")
2908	                assigned_ids.add(eid)
2909	                shown += 1
2910	        if len(ev_ids) > max_outline_ev:
2911	            parts.append(f"  ... +{len(ev_ids) - shown} more evidence assigned to this cluster")
2912	            assigned_ids.update(ev_ids[max_outline_ev:])
2913	
2914	        parts.append("")
2915	
2916	    # Show unassigned evidence (not in any cluster) — also capped
2917	    max_unassigned_display = max_outline_ev  # FIX-2: Same cap for unassigned
2918	    unassigned = [
2919	        e for e in evidence
2920	        if e.get("evidence_id", "") not in assigned_ids
2921	    ]
2922	    if unassigned:
2923	        parts.append(f"## Unassigned evidence ({len(unassigned)} pieces)")
2924	        for e in unassigned[:max_unassigned_display]:
2925	            eid = e.get("evidence_id", "?")
2926	            quality = e.get("quality_tier", "?")
2927	            stmt = e.get("statement", "")[:120]
2928	            parts.append(f"  - [{eid}] ({quality}) {stmt}")
2929	        if len(unassigned) > max_unassigned_display:
2930	            parts.append(f"  ... +{len(unassigned) - max_unassigned_display} more")
2931	        parts.append("")
2932	
2933	    return "\n".join(parts)
2934	
2935	
2936	def _format_cluster_summary(clusters: list[dict]) -> str:
2937	    """FIX-OUTLINE: Format clusters as numbered summaries for the outline prompt (~2KB).
2938	
2939	    Includes cluster numbers so the LLM can reference which clusters
2940	    each section should draw from (e.g., "Covers clusters 1, 3, 7").
2941	    """
2942	    lines = []
2943	    for i, c in enumerate(clusters, 1):
2944	        n_evidence = len(c.get("evidence_ids", []))
2945	        strength = c.get("strength", "moderate")
2946	        lines.append(
2947	            f"Cluster {i}: '{c.get('theme', '?')}' "
2948	            f"({n_evidence} evidence, strength={strength})\n"
2949	            f"  {c.get('description', '')[:200]}"
2950	        )
2951	    return "\n".join(lines) if lines else "No clusters available."

2954	_COT_LINE_PATTERNS = [
2955	    # Lines starting with planning/reasoning markers
2956	    re.compile(r"(?i)^(let me|i need to|i should|i will|the user wants|thinking).*$", re.MULTILINE),
2957	    re.compile(r"(?i)^(word count|checking|verifying|expanding|let's|ok,|alright,).*$", re.MULTILINE),
2958	    re.compile(r"(?i)^(draft \d|attempt \d|revision|re-?writing|here'?s my).*$", re.MULTILINE),
2959	    re.compile(r"(?i)^(wait,|hmm|note:|caveat:|actually,).*$", re.MULTILINE),
2960	    # Lines containing meta-commentary about writing the section
2961	    re.compile(r"^.*(?:word count|check:? need|that's about \d+ words|approximately \d+ words).*$", re.MULTILINE | re.IGNORECASE),
2962	    re.compile(r"^.*(?:need to expand|need a bit more|need more on).*$", re.MULTILINE | re.IGNORECASE),
2963	    re.compile(r"^.*(?:this looks to be around|looks to be about).*$", re.MULTILINE | re.IGNORECASE),
2964	    # XML-style tags
2965	    re.compile(r"<think>.*?</think>", re.DOTALL),
2966	    re.compile(r"<reasoning>.*?</reasoning>", re.DOTALL),
2967	    # --- Enhanced patterns for misrouted reasoning_content ---
2968	    # Numbered instructions (echoed system prompt)
2969	    re.compile(r"^\d+\.\s+(?:Write|Every|Use|Include|Target|Maintain|Connect|Do NOT|No chain|Keep)\b.*$", re.MULTILINE | re.IGNORECASE),
2970	    # Evidence ID listings (bullet points with [ev_xxx])
2971	    re.compile(r"^-\s+.*\[ev_[a-f0-9]+.*$", re.MULTILINE),
2972	    # Per-paragraph word count tracking ("Para 1: ~40 words")
2973	    re.compile(r"^(?:Paragraph|Para)\s+\d+:.*\d+\s+words.*$", re.MULTILINE | re.IGNORECASE),
2974	    # Planning headers
2975	    re.compile(r"^(?:Structure of the section|Available evidence|Key requirements|Output format):?.*$", re.MULTILINE | re.IGNORECASE),
2976	    # Planning bullet points
2977	    re.compile(r"^-\s+(?:Not |Keep |Ensure |Make sure|Don't ).*$", re.MULTILINE),
2978	]

2981	def _deduplicate_drafts(text: str) -> str:
2982	    """Detect and remove duplicate drafts in misrouted reasoning_content.
2983	
2984	    When reasoning_content is used as content, the LLM often writes
2985	    Draft 1 -> word counts -> Draft 2 (improved). Both drafts share
2986	    near-identical opening sentences. Keep only the LAST draft.
2987	    """
2988	    paragraphs = text.split("\n\n")
2989	    if len(paragraphs) < 4:
2990	        return text
2991	
2992	    # Extract first ~60 chars of each paragraph (normalized)
2993	    def _sig(para: str) -> str:
2994	        return re.sub(r"\s+", " ", para.strip()[:60]).lower()
2995	
2996	    # Find paragraphs with matching signatures
2997	    sigs: dict[str, list[int]] = {}
2998	    for i, p in enumerate(paragraphs):
2999	        sig = _sig(p)
3000	        if len(sig) < 30:
3001	            continue
3002	        if sig in sigs:
3003	            sigs[sig].append(i)
3004	        else:
3005	            sigs[sig] = [i]
3006	
3007	    # If any paragraph appears twice AND they're separated by other content,
3008	    # we likely have multiple drafts. Require the duplicates to be separated
3009	    # by at least 1 intervening paragraph.
3010	    dup_with_gap = False
3011	    for indices in sigs.values():
3012	        if len(indices) > 1 and (indices[-1] - indices[0]) >= 2:
3013	            dup_with_gap = True
3014	            break
3015	
3016	    if not dup_with_gap:
3017	        return text
3018	
3019	    # Find the boundary: last occurrence of the first duplicated paragraph
3020	    first_dup_sig = None
3021	    for sig, indices in sigs.items():
3022	        if len(indices) > 1:
3023	            first_dup_sig = sig
3024	            break
3025	
3026	    if first_dup_sig is None:
3027	        return text
3028	
3029	    last_start = sigs[first_dup_sig][-1]
3030	
3031	    # Keep everything from the last draft onwards
3032	    deduped = "\n\n".join(paragraphs[last_start:])
3033	    removed_words = len(text.split()) - len(deduped.split())
3034	
3035	    if removed_words > 0:
3036	        logger.info(
3037	            "[polaris graph] Draft dedup: removed %d words (earlier draft)",
3038	            removed_words,
3039	        )
3040	
3041	    return deduped
3042

3044	def _scrub_cot(text: str) -> str:
3045	    """Remove chain-of-thought leakage from section content."""
3046	    original_len = len(text.split())
3047	    cleaned = text
3048	
3049	    # Step 1: Remove duplicate drafts (must come BEFORE line-level scrubbing)
3050	    cleaned = _deduplicate_drafts(cleaned)
3051	
3052	    # Step 1b: FIX-ESG — Remove broken "Evidence suggests that" mid-sentence injections.
3053	    # GLM-5 writes patterns like "ketone bodies—including Evidence suggests that β-hydroxybutyrate"
3054	    # where "Evidence suggests that" is inserted between a dash/comma and a continuation.
3055	    _esg_pattern = re.compile(
3056	        r'(\u2014|\u2013|-|,|;)(\s*(?:including\s+)?)Evidence suggests that\s+',
3057	        re.IGNORECASE,
3058	    )
3059	    _esg_count = len(_esg_pattern.findall(cleaned))
3060	    if _esg_count:
3061	        cleaned = _esg_pattern.sub(r'\1\2', cleaned)
3062	    # Also handle: "[word] Evidence suggests that [lowercase word]" (no dash)
3063	    _esg_pattern2 = re.compile(
3064	        r'(\w)\s+Evidence suggests that\s+([a-z])',
3065	    )
3066	    _esg_count2 = len(_esg_pattern2.findall(cleaned))
3067	    if _esg_count2:
3068	        cleaned = _esg_pattern2.sub(r'\1 \2', cleaned)
3069	    _esg_total = _esg_count + _esg_count2
3070	    if _esg_total:
3071	        logger.info(
3072	            "[polaris graph] FIX-ESG: Removed %d broken 'Evidence suggests that' mid-sentence injections",
3073	            _esg_total,
3074	        )
3075	
3076	    # Step 2: Apply line-level CoT patterns
3077	    for pattern in _COT_LINE_PATTERNS:
3078	        cleaned = pattern.sub("", cleaned)
3079	
3080	    # Remove excessive blank lines created by scrubbing
3081	    cleaned = re.sub(r"\n{3,}", "\n\n", cleaned).strip()
3082	
3083	    scrubbed_len = len(cleaned.split())
3084	    if scrubbed_len < original_len * 0.3:
3085	        # If scrubbing removed >70% of content, something went wrong —
3086	        # return original to avoid destroying the section
3087	        logger.warning(
3088	            "[polaris graph] CoT scrub removed >70%% (%d->%d words), keeping original",
3089	            original_len,
3090	            scrubbed_len,
3091	        )
3092	        return text
3093	
3094	    if scrubbed_len < original_len:
3095	        logger.info(
3096	            "[polaris graph] CoT scrub: %d -> %d words (-%d)",
3097	            original_len,
3098	            scrubbed_len,
3099	            original_len - scrubbed_len,
3100	        )
3101	
3102	    return cleaned
3103

3105	def _clean_artifacts(text: str, section_titles: list[str] | None = None) -> str:
3106	    """FIX-059-C: Remove LLM artifacts that leak through generation and post-processing.
3107	
3108	    Handles:
3109	    1. Orphan transition fragments (e.g. "Additionally,." left after citation cleanup)
3110	    2. [CROSS-REF:...] markers not resolved during assembly
3111	    3. Section [X]/[Y]/[Z] placeholder references
3112	    4. Title echo (section body starting with its own title)
3113	    5. Markdown headers from expansion output (H-06)
3114	    6. Double spaces and empty lines
3115	
3116	    Args:
3117	        text: The section or report text to clean.
3118	        section_titles: Optional list of section titles to detect title echo.
3119	
3120	    Returns:
3121	        Cleaned text with artifacts removed.
3122	    """
3123	    if not text or not text.strip():
3124	        return text
3125	
3126	    cleaned = text
3127	
3128	    # 1. Strip orphan transition fragments: "Additionally,." or "Moreover."
3129	    #    These arise when _inject_transitions() adds a transition word before
3130	    #    a sentence that is later stripped during citation orphan cleanup,
3131	    #    leaving the transition word dangling with only punctuation.
3132	    cleaned = re.sub(
3133	        r'\b(Additionally|Moreover|Furthermore|In addition|Consequently|'
3134	        r'Specifically|Notably|Importantly|Critically|Similarly|Likewise|'
3135	        r'Conversely)[,.]?\.\s*',
3136	        '',
3137	        cleaned,
3138	    )
3139	
3140	    # 2. Strip [CROSS-REF:...] markers
3141	    cleaned = re.sub(r'\[CROSS-REF:[^\]]*\]', '', cleaned)
3142	
3143	    # 3. Strip Section [X]/[Y]/[Z] placeholder references and clean up
3144	    #    resulting empty sentences
3145	    cleaned = re.sub(r'(?:Section|section)\s+\[[A-Z]\]', '', cleaned)
3146	    # Clean up sentences that became empty after stripping placeholders
3147	    # e.g. "As discussed in ." -> remove entire empty sentence
3148	    cleaned = re.sub(r'(?<=[.!?])\s+[.!?]', '', cleaned)
3149	
3150	    # 4. Remove title echo: if body starts with the exact section title
3151	    if section_titles:
3152	        for title in section_titles:
3153	            if not title:
3154	                continue
3155	            # Case-insensitive match at start of text (with optional markdown header)
3156	            pattern = re.compile(
3157	                r'^(?:#{1,4}\s+)?' + re.escape(title) + r'\s*\n?',
3158	                re.IGNORECASE,
3159	            )
3160	            cleaned = pattern.sub('', cleaned, count=1)
3161	
3162	    # 5. Strip markdown headers from expansion output (H-06)
3163	    #    Expansion LLM sometimes outputs "## Subsection Title" headers
3164	    cleaned = re.sub(r'^#{1,4}\s+.*$', '', cleaned, flags=re.MULTILINE)
3165	
3166	    # 6. Final cleanup: double spaces, excessive blank lines
3167	    cleaned = re.sub(r'  +', ' ', cleaned)
3168	    cleaned = re.sub(r"\n{3,}", "\n\n", cleaned)
3169	    cleaned = cleaned.strip()
3170	
3171	    if len(cleaned) < len(text.strip()):
3172	        logger.info(
3173	            "[polaris graph] FIX-059-C: _clean_artifacts() removed %d chars",
3174	            len(text.strip()) - len(cleaned),
3175	        )
3176	
3177	    return cleaned
3178

3181	def _limit_hedging(text: str) -> str:
3182	    """FIX-R13: Post-processing enforcement of hedging word limits.
3183	
3184	    Prompt instructions alone don't reliably limit hedging. This function
3185	    enforces a hard cap by replacing excess hedging words with confident
3186	    alternatives.
3187	    """
3188	    if not text or not text.strip():
3189	        return text
3190	
3191	    max_hedging = int(os.getenv("PG_MAX_HEDGING_PER_SECTION", "4"))
3192	
3193	    # Pattern matches common hedging words/phrases
3194	    _hedging_pattern = re.compile(
3195	        r'\b(may|might|potentially|possibly|could|perhaps|appears to|seems to)\b',
3196	        re.IGNORECASE,
3197	    )
3198	
3199	    matches = list(_hedging_pattern.finditer(text))
3200	    if len(matches) <= max_hedging:
3201	        return text
3202	
3203	    # Keep the first max_hedging instances, remove/replace the rest
3204	    excess = matches[max_hedging:]
3205	    # Process in reverse order to preserve string positions
3206	    result = text
3207	    removed = 0
3208	    for match in reversed(excess):
3209	        word = match.group(0).lower()
3210	        start, end = match.start(), match.end()
3211	
3212	        if word in ("potentially", "possibly", "perhaps"):
3213	            # Simply remove: "potentially harmful" -> "harmful"
3214	            # Also remove trailing space
3215	            if end < len(result) and result[end] == ' ':
3216	                result = result[:start] + result[end + 1:]
3217	            else:
3218	                result = result[:start] + result[end:]
3219	            removed += 1
3220	        elif word in ("may", "might"):
3221	            # "may cause" -> "causes" (simplified: just remove "may "/"might ")
3222	            if end < len(result) and result[end] == ' ':
3223	                result = result[:start] + result[end + 1:]
3224	            else:
3225	                result = result[:start] + result[end:]
3226	            removed += 1
3227	        elif word == "could":
3228	            # "could affect" -> "can affect"
3229	            result = result[:start] + "can" + result[end:]
3230	            removed += 1
3231	        elif word in ("appears to", "seems to"):
3232	            # Remove entirely
3233	            if end < len(result) and result[end] == ' ':
3234	                result = result[:start] + result[end + 1:]
3235	            else:
3236	                result = result[:start] + result[end:]
3237	            removed += 1
3238	
3239	    # Re-capitalize sentence starts that may have been affected
3240	    result = re.sub(r'(\. +)([a-z])', lambda m: m.group(1) + m.group(2).upper(), result)
3241	    # Clean double spaces
3242	    result = re.sub(r'  +', ' ', result)
3243	
3244	    if removed > 0:
3245	        logger.info(
3246	            "[polaris graph] FIX-R13: Removed/replaced %d/%d excess hedging words "
3247	            "(max %d per section)",
3248	            removed, len(matches), max_hedging,
3249	        )
3250	
3251	    return result
3252

3254	def _limit_transitions(text: str) -> str:
3255	    """FIX-059-K: Limit transition word density to prevent over-saturation.
3256	
3257	    LLM writes transitions + post-processor adds more, causing 1 transition
3258	    every 81 words (129 in 10,500 words). This function enforces a cap of
3259	    ~60 per 10,000 words by removing transitions from middle paragraphs
3260	    when density exceeds 1 per 150 words.
3261	
3262	    Args:
3263	        text: Section content text.
3264	
3265	    Returns:
3266	        Text with transition density capped.
3267	    """
3268	    if not text or not text.strip():
3269	        return text
3270	
3271	    # LAW VI: Thresholds from environment
3272	    max_density = float(os.getenv("PG_TRANSITION_MAX_DENSITY", "150"))  # 1 per N words
3273	    cap_per_10k = int(os.getenv("PG_TRANSITION_CAP_PER_10K", "60"))
3274	
3275	    _transition_words = [
3276	        "Additionally", "Moreover", "Furthermore", "In addition",
3277	        "Consequently", "Specifically", "Notably", "Importantly",
3278	        "Similarly", "Likewise", "Conversely", "However",
3279	        "Nevertheless", "Nonetheless",
3280	    ]
3281	    _transition_pattern = re.compile(
3282	        r"\b(" + "|".join(_transition_words) + r")\b",
3283	        re.IGNORECASE,
3284	    )
3285	
3286	    word_count = len(text.split())
3287	    if word_count == 0:
3288	        return text
3289	
3290	    all_matches = list(_transition_pattern.finditer(text))
3291	    total_transitions = len(all_matches)
3292	
3293	    if total_transitions == 0:
3294	        return text
3295	
3296	    # Check if density exceeds threshold
3297	    density_words_per_transition = word_count / total_transitions
3298	    scaled_cap = max(1, int(cap_per_10k * word_count / 10000))
3299	
3300	    if density_words_per_transition >= max_density and total_transitions <= scaled_cap:
3301	        return text  # Already within limits
3302	
3303	    # Split into paragraphs; keep first and last paragraph intact
3304	    paragraphs = text.split("\n\n")
3305	    if len(paragraphs) <= 2:
3306	        return text  # Only first/last -- nothing to trim
3307	
3308	    target_removals = max(0, total_transitions - scaled_cap)
3309	    if target_removals == 0:
3310	        return text
3311	
3312	    removed = 0
3313	    result_paragraphs = []
3314	    for idx, para in enumerate(paragraphs):
3315	        # Keep first and last paragraphs unchanged
3316	        if idx == 0 or idx == len(paragraphs) - 1:
3317	            result_paragraphs.append(para)
3318	            continue
3319	
3320	        if removed >= target_removals:
3321	            result_paragraphs.append(para)
3322	            continue
3323	
3324	        # Remove transition words at sentence starts in middle paragraphs
3325	        def _strip_transition(match, _para=para):
3326	            nonlocal removed
3327	            if removed >= target_removals:
3328	                return match.group(0)
3329	            # Only strip if transition starts a sentence (preceded by '. ' or start-of-para)
3330	            start = match.start()
3331	            pre = _para[:start].rstrip()
3332	            if not pre or pre.endswith(".") or pre.endswith("?") or pre.endswith("!"):
3333	                removed += 1
3334	                return ""
3335	            return match.group(0)
3336	
3337	        cleaned_para = _transition_pattern.sub(_strip_transition, para)
3338	        # Clean up artifacts: leading comma/space, double spaces
3339	        cleaned_para = re.sub(r"^[,\s]+", "", cleaned_para)
3340	        cleaned_para = re.sub(r"(?<=\.)\s+[,]\s+", " ", cleaned_para)
3341	        cleaned_para = re.sub(r"  +", " ", cleaned_para)
3342	        # Re-capitalize first letter after removal if needed
3343	        if cleaned_para and cleaned_para[0].islower():
3344	            cleaned_para = cleaned_para[0].upper() + cleaned_para[1:]
3345	        result_paragraphs.append(cleaned_para)
3346	
3347	    result = "\n\n".join(result_paragraphs)
3348	
3349	    # FIX-R3: Capitalize sentence starts after transition removal
3350	    # Split by paragraph, check each paragraph's first char
3351	    _r3_paragraphs = result.split("\n\n")
3352	    _r3_fixed = []
3353	    for _r3_p in _r3_paragraphs:
3354	        _r3_p = _r3_p.strip()
3355	        if _r3_p and _r3_p[0].islower():
3356	            _r3_p = _r3_p[0].upper() + _r3_p[1:]
3357	        # Also fix sentence starts within paragraph (after ". ")
3358	        _r3_p = re.sub(r'(\.\s+)([a-z])', lambda m: m.group(1) + m.group(2).upper(), _r3_p)
3359	        _r3_fixed.append(_r3_p)
3360	    result = "\n\n".join(_r3_fixed)
3361	
3362	    if removed > 0:
3363	        logger.info(
3364	            "[polaris graph] FIX-059-K: Removed %d/%d transition words "
3365	            "(density: 1/%.0f words -> 1/%.0f words, cap: %d per 10K)",
3366	            removed,
3367	            total_transitions,
3368	            density_words_per_transition,
3369	            word_count / max(total_transitions - removed, 1),
3370	            scaled_cap,
3371	        )
3372	
3373	    return result
3374

3376	def _break_long_paragraphs(text: str) -> str:
3377	    """FIX-059-L: Break excessively long paragraphs for readability.
3378	
3379	    Splits any paragraph exceeding 300 words at the nearest sentence boundary
3380	    after approximately 250 words. Uses regex-based sentence splitting to
3381	    avoid adding new dependencies.
3382	
3383	    Args:
3384	        text: Section content text.
3385	
3386	    Returns:
3387	        Text with long paragraphs broken into smaller ones.
3388	    """
3389	    if not text or not text.strip():
3390	        return text
3391	
3392	    # LAW VI: Threshold from environment
3393	    max_paragraph_words = int(os.getenv("PG_MAX_PARAGRAPH_WORDS", "300"))
3394	    split_target_words = int(os.getenv("PG_PARAGRAPH_SPLIT_TARGET", "250"))
3395	
3396	    paragraphs = text.split("\n\n")
3397	    result_paragraphs = []
3398	    splits_made = 0
3399	
3400	    for para in paragraphs:
3401	        words = para.split()
3402	        if len(words) <= max_paragraph_words:
3403	            result_paragraphs.append(para)
3404	            continue
3405	
3406	        # Split at sentence boundaries using regex
3407	        # Handles abbreviations like U.S., Dr., Prof., e.g., i.e. by requiring
3408	        # the period to be followed by a space and an uppercase letter or [
3409	        sentences = re.split(r'(?<=[.!?])\s+(?=[A-Z\[])', para)
3410	
3411	        if len(sentences) <= 1:
3412	            result_paragraphs.append(para)
3413	            continue
3414	
3415	        current_chunk: list[str] = []
3416	        current_word_count = 0
3417	
3418	        for sent in sentences:
3419	            sent_words = len(sent.split())
3420	            if current_word_count + sent_words > split_target_words and current_chunk:
3421	                result_paragraphs.append(" ".join(current_chunk))
3422	                current_chunk = [sent]
3423	                current_word_count = sent_words
3424	                splits_made += 1
3425	            else:
3426	                current_chunk.append(sent)
3427	                current_word_count += sent_words
3428	
3429	        if current_chunk:
3430	            result_paragraphs.append(" ".join(current_chunk))
3431	
3432	        # FIX-R5: Clause-level split for chunks still exceeding 300 words
3433	        # After sentence splitting, some chunks may still be too long
3434	        # (e.g., single very long sentences). Split at clause boundaries.
3435	        _r5_final = []
3436	        for _r5_chunk in result_paragraphs[len(result_paragraphs) - splits_made - 1:]:
3437	            if len(_r5_chunk.split()) > max_paragraph_words:
3438	                _r5_clauses = re.split(
3439	                    r'(?<=; )(?=[A-Z])|(?<=, which )|(?<=, and )(?=[A-Z])|(?<=, but )(?=[A-Z])',
3440	                    _r5_chunk,
3441	                )
3442	                if len(_r5_clauses) > 1:
3443	                    _r5_sub_chunk: list[str] = []
3444	                    _r5_sub_wc = 0
3445	                    for _r5_cl in _r5_clauses:
3446	                        _r5_cl_wc = len(_r5_cl.split())
3447	                        if _r5_sub_wc + _r5_cl_wc > split_target_words and _r5_sub_chunk:
3448	                            _r5_final.append("".join(_r5_sub_chunk))
3449	                            _r5_sub_chunk = [_r5_cl]
3450	                            _r5_sub_wc = _r5_cl_wc
3451	                            splits_made += 1
3452	                        else:
3453	                            _r5_sub_chunk.append(_r5_cl)
3454	                            _r5_sub_wc += _r5_cl_wc
3455	                    if _r5_sub_chunk:
3456	                        _r5_final.append("".join(_r5_sub_chunk))
3457	                else:
3458	                    _r5_final.append(_r5_chunk)
3459	            else:
3460	                _r5_final.append(_r5_chunk)
3461	        # Replace the tail of result_paragraphs with clause-split versions
3462	        if _r5_final:
3463	            _r5_start = len(result_paragraphs) - splits_made - 1
3464	            if _r5_start >= 0:
3465	                result_paragraphs = result_paragraphs[:_r5_start] + _r5_final
3466	
3467	
3468	    result = "\n\n".join(result_paragraphs)
3469	
3470	    if splits_made > 0:
3471	        logger.info(
3472	            "[polaris graph] FIX-059-L: Broke %d long paragraph(s) at sentence boundaries",
3473	            splits_made,
3474	        )
3475	
3476	    return result
3477	
3478	
3479	def _format_evidence_for_writing(evidence: list[EvidencePiece]) -> str:
3480	    """Format evidence for section writing (detailed).
3481	
3482	    FIX-5: Sorts evidence by tier (GOLD first) then relevance DESC so the
3483	    LLM sees the strongest evidence first and prioritizes it.
3484	    FIX-MP4: Adds [VERIFIED]/[UNVERIFIED] tags based on verification status.
3485	    FIX-MP16: Includes direct_quote to give LLM actual source text for
3486	    tighter claim-evidence coupling (ReClaim-lite approach).
3487	    """
3488	    # FIX-5: Sort by quality tier (GOLD=0, SILVER=1, BRONZE=2) then relevance DESC
3489	    _tier_rank = {"GOLD": 0, "SILVER": 1, "BRONZE": 2}
3490	    sorted_evidence = sorted(
3491	        evidence,
3492	        key=lambda e: (
3493	            _tier_rank.get(e.get("quality_tier", "BRONZE"), 2),
3494	            -(e.get("relevance_score", 0.0)),
3495	        ),
3496	    )
3497	
3498	    lines = []
3499	    _current_tier = None
3500	    for e in sorted_evidence:
3501	        # FIX-5: Add tier header when tier changes
3502	        _tier = e.get("quality_tier", "BRONZE")
3503	        if _tier != _current_tier:
3504	            _current_tier = _tier
3505	            lines.append(f"--- {_tier} EVIDENCE (sorted by relevance) ---")
3506	
3507	        # FIX-MP4: Verification status tag
3508	        verification_method = e.get("verification_method", "")
3509	        if verification_method == "api_error" or verification_method == "":
3510	            verify_tag = "[UNVERIFIED]"
3511	        elif e.get("is_faithful") is True:
3512	            verify_tag = "[VERIFIED]"
3513	        elif e.get("is_faithful") is False:
3514	            verify_tag = "[UNVERIFIED]"
3515	        else:
3516	            verify_tag = "[UNVERIFIED]"
3517	
3518	        # FIX-MP16: Include full direct_quote for source grounding
3519	        quote = e.get("direct_quote", "")
3520	        quote_line = f'  Direct quote: "{quote[:500]}"' if quote else ""
3521	
3522	        lines.append(
3523	            f"Evidence ID: {e.get('evidence_id', '?')} {verify_tag}\n"
3524	            f"  Statement: {e.get('statement', '')}\n"
3525	            f"{quote_line}\n"
3526	            f"  Source: {e.get('source_title', '')} ({e.get('year', '?')})\n"
3527	            f"  URL: {e.get('source_url', '')}\n"
3528	            f"  Quality: {e.get('quality_tier', '?')} | Relevance: {e.get('relevance_score', 0):.2f}\n"
3529	        )
3530	    return "\n".join(lines) if lines else "No evidence assigned to this section."
