1	"""
2	SmartArt diagram generator for polaris graph reports (Amendment A5).
3	
4	Analyzes completed report sections to identify which benefit from visual
5	diagrams, then generates content-level Mermaid.js code grounded in the
6	section text and supporting evidence. All diagram facts are traceable to
7	the source material — no invented data.
8	
9	Entry point: SmartArtGenerator.generate_smart_art_for_report()
10	"""
11	
12	import logging
13	import os
14	import re
15	from typing import Optional
16	
17	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, field_validator
18	
19	from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient
20	from src.polaris_graph.tracing import get_tracer
21	
22	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
23	
24	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
25	# Configuration (LAW VI — zero hard-coding)
26	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
27	
28	PG_MAX_SMART_ART: int = int(os.getenv("PG_MAX_SMART_ART", "5"))
29	PG_SMART_ART_ENABLED: bool = os.getenv("PG_SMART_ART_ENABLED", "1") == "1"
30	
31	# LLM call parameters — configurable via environment
32	_ANALYSIS_MAX_TOKENS: int = int(os.getenv("PG_SMART_ART_ANALYSIS_MAX_TOKENS", "4096"))
33	_MERMAID_MAX_TOKENS: int = int(os.getenv("PG_SMART_ART_MERMAID_MAX_TOKENS", "4096"))
34	_ANALYSIS_TEMPERATURE: float = float(os.getenv("PG_SMART_ART_ANALYSIS_TEMP", "0.4"))
35	_MERMAID_TEMPERATURE: float = float(os.getenv("PG_SMART_ART_MERMAID_TEMP", "0.3"))
36	_ANALYSIS_TIMEOUT: float = float(os.getenv("PG_SMART_ART_ANALYSIS_TIMEOUT", "120"))
37	_MERMAID_TIMEOUT: float = float(os.getenv("PG_SMART_ART_MERMAID_TIMEOUT", "90"))
38	
39	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
40	# Diagram type registry
41	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
42	
43	DIAGRAM_TYPES: dict[str, str] = {
44	    "process_flow": "How X works (step-by-step process)",
45	    "comparison_matrix": "X vs Y vs Z comparison",
46	    "causal_chain": "A causes B which leads to C",
47	    "hierarchy": "Classification/taxonomy of findings",
48	    "timeline": "Chronological development of topic",
49	    "pros_cons": "Advantages vs disadvantages",
50	    "decision_tree": "If X then Y, else Z",
51	}
52	
53	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
54	# Pydantic schemas for structured LLM output
55	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
56	
57	
58	class DiagramRecommendation(BaseModel):
59	    """A single recommendation for a diagram to add to a report section."""
60	
61	    section_id: str = Field(description="The section_id this diagram targets")
62	    section_title: str = Field(description="Title of the targeted section")
63	    diagram_type: str = Field(description="One of the supported DIAGRAM_TYPES keys")
64	    description: str = Field(
65	        description="What the diagram should show, grounded in the section content"
66	    )
67	
68	    @field_validator("diagram_type")
69	    @classmethod
70	    def validate_diagram_type(cls, v: str) -> str:
71	        """Coerce to closest valid type; reject unknowns."""
72	        normalised = v.strip().lower().replace("-", "_").replace(" ", "_")
73	        if normalised in DIAGRAM_TYPES:
74	            return normalised
75	        # Attempt fuzzy match on prefix
76	        for key in DIAGRAM_TYPES:
77	            if normalised.startswith(key[:6]):
78	                logger.debug(
79	                    "[smart_art] Coerced diagram_type '%s' -> '%s'", v, key
80	                )
81	                return key
82	        logger.warning(
83	            "[smart_art] Unknown diagram_type '%s', defaulting to 'process_flow'", v
84	        )
85	        return "process_flow"
86	
87	
88	class DiagramAnalysisResult(BaseModel):
89	    """Full analysis output: which sections deserve diagrams."""
90	
91	    recommendations: list[DiagramRecommendation] = Field(
92	        default_factory=list,
93	        description="2-5 sections that would benefit from a visual diagram",
94	    )
95	
96	
97	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
98	# Mermaid validation helpers
99	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
100	
101	# Valid Mermaid diagram start tokens (non-exhaustive but covers common types)
102	_MERMAID_START_PATTERNS = [
103	    "graph ",
104	    "graph\n",
105	    "flowchart ",
106	    "flowchart\n",
107	    "sequenceDiagram",
108	    "classDiagram",
109	    "stateDiagram",
110	    "erDiagram",
111	    "gantt",
112	    "pie",
113	    "journey",
114	    "gitgraph",
115	    "mindmap",
116	    "timeline",
117	    "xychart",
118	    "block-beta",
119	    "sankey-beta",
120	    "quadrantChart",
121	]
122	
123	_CODE_FENCE_RE = re.compile(
124	    r"^```(?:mermaid|mmd|text)?\s*\n(.*?)```\s*$",
125	    re.DOTALL | re.MULTILINE,
126	)
127	
128	
129	def _strip_code_fences(raw: str) -> str:
130	    """Remove markdown code fences wrapping Mermaid code."""
131	    text = raw.strip()
132	    match = _CODE_FENCE_RE.search(text)
133	    if match:
134	        return match.group(1).strip()
135	    # Also handle single-line fence at start/end
136	    if text.startswith("```"):
137	        lines = text.split("\n")
138	        # Drop first line (```mermaid) and last line (```)
139	        inner_lines = []
140	        started = False
141	        for line in lines:
142	            if not started and line.strip().startswith("```"):
143	                started = True
144	                continue
145	            if started and line.strip() == "```":
146	                break
147	            if started:
148	                inner_lines.append(line)
149	        if inner_lines:
150	            return "\n".join(inner_lines).strip()
151	    return text
152	
153	
154	def _validate_mermaid(code: str) -> bool:
155	    """
156	    Basic structural validation for Mermaid.js code.
157	
158	    Checks that the code begins with a recognised diagram declaration
159	    and contains at least one relationship or node definition. This is
160	    intentionally lenient — full rendering validation is left to the
161	    client-side Mermaid renderer.
162	    """
163	    if not code or len(code.strip()) < 10:
164	        return False
165	
166	    normalised = code.strip().lower()
167	    has_valid_start = any(
168	        normalised.startswith(pattern.lower()) for pattern in _MERMAID_START_PATTERNS
169	    )
170	    if not has_valid_start:
171	        logger.debug(
172	            "[smart_art] Mermaid validation failed: unrecognised start token. "
173	            "First 60 chars: '%s'",
174	            code.strip()[:60],
175	        )
176	        return False
177	
178	    # Must have at least two non-empty lines (declaration + content)
179	    meaningful_lines = [
180	        line for line in code.strip().split("\n") if line.strip()
181	    ]
182	    if len(meaningful_lines) < 2:
183	        logger.debug("[smart_art] Mermaid validation failed: fewer than 2 lines")
184	        return False
185	
186	    return True
187	
188	
189	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
190	# Prompt templates
191	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
192	
193	_ANALYSIS_SYSTEM_PROMPT = (
194	    "You are a research report visualization specialist. "
195	    "You identify which report sections benefit most from visual diagrams "
196	    "and select the appropriate diagram type for each.\n\n"
197	    "Available diagram types:\n"
198	    + "\n".join(f'- "{k}": {v}' for k, v in DIAGRAM_TYPES.items())
199	    + "\n\n"
200	    "Output format example:\n"
201	    '{"recommendations": [{"section_id": "s01", "section_title": "Mechanisms of Action", '
202	    '"diagram_type": "process_flow", "description": "Step-by-step process of how compound X '
203	    'binds to receptor Y, triggers pathway Z, and produces effect W as described in the section"}]}'
204	)
205	
206	_ANALYSIS_PROMPT_TEMPLATE = (
207	    "Given these report sections, identify 2-5 that would benefit from a "
208	    "visual diagram. For each, specify the diagram type and what the diagram "
209	    "should show.\n\n"
210	    "SECTIONS:\n{sections_text}\n\n"
211	    "Constraints:\n"
212	    "- Select at most {max_diagrams} sections.\n"
213	    "- Only recommend a diagram if it adds genuine clarity beyond the text.\n"
214	    "- The description must reference specific facts or structures present in "
215	    "the section content.\n"
216	    "- Choose the diagram type that best fits the section's analytical structure."
217	)
218	
219	_MERMAID_SYSTEM_PROMPT = (
220	    "You are a Mermaid.js diagram expert. "
221	    "You produce syntactically correct Mermaid code that visualizes "
222	    "research findings accurately. "
223	    "Output ONLY the raw Mermaid code — no markdown fences, no explanation, "
224	    "no commentary."
225	)
226	
227	_MERMAID_PROMPT_TEMPLATE = (
228	    "Given this research section about {topic}, generate a {diagram_type} "
229	    "Mermaid.js diagram. Use ONLY facts stated in the text. Do not invent "
230	    "data. Include citation references [N] on relevant nodes where applicable. "
231	    "Return ONLY the Mermaid code, no explanation.\n\n"
232	    "Diagram type description: {diagram_description}\n\n"
233	    "SECTION CONTENT:\n{section_content}\n\n"
234	    "SUPPORTING EVIDENCE SUMMARIES:\n{evidence_summaries}"
235	)
236	
237	
238	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
239	# SmartArtGenerator
240	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
241	
242	
243	class SmartArtGenerator:
244	    """
245	    Generates content-level Mermaid.js diagrams for research report sections.
246	
247	    Usage:
248	        generator = SmartArtGenerator()
249	        diagrams = await generator.generate_smart_art_for_report(
250	            sections=report_sections,
251	            evidence=all_evidence,
252	            llm_client=openrouter_client,
253	        )
254	        # diagrams: {section_id: mermaid_code_string, ...}
255	    """
256	
257	    def __init__(self) -> None:
258	        self._tracer = get_tracer()
259	
260	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
261	    # Public API
262	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
263	
264	    async def analyze_sections(
265	        self,
266	        sections: list[dict],
267	        llm_client: OpenRouterClient,
268	    ) -> list[dict]:
269	        """
270	        Identify which sections (2-5) would benefit from a visual diagram.
271	
272	        Parameters
273	        ----------
274	        sections : list[dict]
275	            Report sections, each with at minimum ``section_id``, ``title``,
276	            and ``content`` keys (matching ``ReportSection`` TypedDict).
277	        llm_client : OpenRouterClient
278	            The shared LLM client instance.
279	
280	        Returns
281	        -------
282	        list[dict]
283	            Each entry: ``{"section_id", "section_title", "diagram_type",
284	            "description"}``. Empty list on total failure.
285	        """
286	        if not sections:
287	            logger.warning("[smart_art] analyze_sections called with empty sections list")
288	            return []
289	
290	        sections_text = self._format_sections_for_analysis(sections)
291	        prompt = _ANALYSIS_PROMPT_TEMPLATE.format(
292	            sections_text=sections_text,
293	            max_diagrams=PG_MAX_SMART_ART,
294	        )
295	
296	        try:
297	            result: DiagramAnalysisResult = await llm_client.generate_structured(
298	                prompt=prompt,
299	                schema=DiagramAnalysisResult,
300	                system=_ANALYSIS_SYSTEM_PROMPT,
301	                max_tokens=_ANALYSIS_MAX_TOKENS,
302	                timeout=_ANALYSIS_TIMEOUT,
303	            )
304	        except Exception as exc:
305	            logger.error(
306	                "[smart_art] LLM analysis call failed: %s", str(exc)[:200]
307	            )
308	            return []
309	
310	        # Validate that recommended section_ids actually exist
311	        valid_ids = {s.get("section_id") or s.get("id", "") for s in sections}
312	        validated: list[dict] = []
313	        for rec in result.recommendations:
314	            if rec.section_id not in valid_ids:
315	                logger.warning(
316	                    "[smart_art] LLM recommended non-existent section_id '%s', skipping",
317	                    rec.section_id,
318	                )
319	                continue
320	            validated.append({
321	                "section_id": rec.section_id,
322	                "section_title": rec.section_title,
323	                "diagram_type": rec.diagram_type,
324	                "description": rec.description,
325	            })
326	
327	        # Enforce cap
328	        if len(validated) > PG_MAX_SMART_ART:
329	            logger.info(
330	                "[smart_art] Capping recommendations from %d to %d",
331	                len(validated),
332	                PG_MAX_SMART_ART,
333	            )
334	            validated = validated[:PG_MAX_SMART_ART]
335	
336	        logger.info(
337	            "[smart_art] Analysis complete: %d sections recommended for diagrams",
338	            len(validated),
339	        )
340	        return validated
341	
342	    async def generate_mermaid(
343	        self,
344	        section_title: str,
345	        section_content: str,
346	        evidence_summaries: list[str],
347	        diagram_type: str,
348	        llm_client: OpenRouterClient,
349	    ) -> str:
350	        """
351	        Generate a Mermaid.js diagram for a single section.
352	
353	        Parameters
354	        ----------
355	        section_title : str
356	            Title of the target section (used as the topic in the prompt).
357	        section_content : str
358	            Full text content of the section.
359	        evidence_summaries : list[str]
360	            Summarised evidence statements supporting this section.
361	        diagram_type : str
362	            One of the keys in ``DIAGRAM_TYPES``.
363	        llm_client : OpenRouterClient
364	            The shared LLM client instance.
365	
366	        Returns
367	        -------
368	        str
369	            Valid Mermaid.js code string. Empty string on failure.
370	        """
371	        if not section_content.strip():
372	            logger.warning(
373	                "[smart_art] generate_mermaid called with empty content for '%s'",
374	                section_title,
375	            )
376	            return ""
377	
378	        diagram_description = DIAGRAM_TYPES.get(diagram_type, DIAGRAM_TYPES["process_flow"])
379	        evidence_text = "\n".join(
380	            f"- {summary}" for summary in evidence_summaries
381	        ) if evidence_summaries else "(No additional evidence summaries provided)"
382	
383	        prompt = _MERMAID_PROMPT_TEMPLATE.format(
384	            topic=section_title,
385	            diagram_type=diagram_type,
386	            diagram_description=diagram_description,
387	            section_content=section_content,
388	            evidence_summaries=evidence_text,
389	        )
390	
391	        try:
392	            response = await llm_client.generate(
393	                prompt=prompt,
394	                system=_MERMAID_SYSTEM_PROMPT,
395	                max_tokens=_MERMAID_MAX_TOKENS,
396	                temperature=_MERMAID_TEMPERATURE,
397	                timeout=_MERMAID_TIMEOUT,
398	            )
399	        except Exception as exc:
400	            logger.error(
401	                "[smart_art] Mermaid generation LLM call failed for '%s': %s",
402	                section_title,
403	                str(exc)[:200],
404	            )
405	            return ""
406	
407	        raw_code = response.content.strip()
408	        if not raw_code:
409	            logger.warning(
410	                "[smart_art] LLM returned empty content for '%s' diagram", section_title
411	            )
412	            return ""
413	
414	        # Strip code fences if present (LLM sometimes wraps in ```mermaid)
415	        mermaid_code = _strip_code_fences(raw_code)
416	
417	        if not _validate_mermaid(mermaid_code):
418	            logger.warning(
419	                "[smart_art] Invalid Mermaid code for section '%s' "
420	                "(type=%s, length=%d). First 120 chars: '%s'",
421	                section_title,
422	                diagram_type,
423	                len(mermaid_code),
424	                mermaid_code[:120],
425	            )
426	            # FIX-CITE-3/A4: Retry with simpler diagram type (flowchart TD)
427	            logger.info(
428	                "[smart_art] FIX-A4: Retrying '%s' with simplified flowchart",
429	                section_title[:40],
430	            )
431	            try:
432	                retry_prompt = (
433	                    f"Create a simple Mermaid.js flowchart (flowchart TD) for:\n"
434	                    f"Section: {section_title}\n"
435	                    f"Use at most 10 nodes. Keep node labels under 40 characters.\n"
436	                    f"Output ONLY valid Mermaid code, no markdown fences."
437	                )
438	                retry_resp = await llm_client.generate(
439	                    prompt=retry_prompt,
440	                    max_tokens=1500,
441	                    temperature=0.3,
442	                )
443	                retry_code = _strip_code_fences(retry_resp.content.strip())
444	                _retry_lines = len(retry_code.strip().split("\n"))
445	                _min = int(os.getenv("PG_DIAGRAM_MIN_LINES", "10"))
446	                if _validate_mermaid(retry_code) and _retry_lines >= _min:
447	                    logger.info(
448	                        "[smart_art] FIX-A4: Retry succeeded for '%s' (%d chars, %d lines)",
449	                        section_title[:40],
450	                        len(retry_code),
451	                        _retry_lines,
452	                    )
453	                    return retry_code
454	                elif _validate_mermaid(retry_code):
455	                    logger.warning(
456	                        "[smart_art] FIX-071: Retry diagram too trivial for '%s' "
457	                        "(%d lines < %d min)",
458	                        section_title[:40], _retry_lines, _min,
459	                    )
460	            except Exception as retry_exc:
461	                logger.warning(
462	                    "[smart_art] FIX-A4: Retry failed for '%s': %s",
463	                    section_title[:40],
464	                    str(retry_exc)[:100],
465	                )
466	            return ""
467	
468	        # FIX-071: Reject trivial diagrams (<10 lines or <200 chars).
469	        # s05 "Healthy Diet → Insulin Sensitivity" (6 lines) and
470	        # s12 "Identify Population → Contraindication?" (6 lines)
471	        # added no value in TEST_071. Minimum 10 lines ensures substance.
472	        _min_lines = int(os.getenv("PG_DIAGRAM_MIN_LINES", "10"))
473	        _diagram_lines = len(mermaid_code.strip().split("\n"))
474	        if _diagram_lines < _min_lines:
475	            logger.warning(
476	                "[smart_art] FIX-071: Rejected trivial diagram for '%s' "
477	                "(%d lines < %d minimum)",
478	                section_title[:40], _diagram_lines, _min_lines,
479	            )
480	            return ""
481	
482	        logger.info(
483	            "[smart_art] Generated %s diagram for '%s' (%d chars, %d lines)",
484	            diagram_type,
485	            section_title,
486	            len(mermaid_code),
487	            _diagram_lines,
488	        )
489	        return mermaid_code
490	
491	    async def generate_smart_art_for_report(
492	        self,
493	        sections: list[dict],
494	        evidence: list[dict],
495	        llm_client: OpenRouterClient,
496	        max_diagrams: int = PG_MAX_SMART_ART,
497	    ) -> dict[str, str]:
498	        """
499	        Main entry point: generate Mermaid.js diagrams for a completed report.
500	
501	        Parameters
502	        ----------
503	        sections : list[dict]
504	            All report sections (``ReportSection`` dicts with ``section_id``,
505	            ``title``, ``content``, and ``evidence_ids``).
506	        evidence : list[dict]
507	            All evidence pieces (``EvidencePiece`` dicts with ``evidence_id``
508	            and ``statement``).
509	        llm_client : OpenRouterClient
510	            The shared LLM client instance.
511	        max_diagrams : int
512	            Maximum number of diagrams to generate (overrides env default).
513	
514	        Returns
515	        -------
516	        dict[str, str]
517	            Mapping of ``section_id`` to Mermaid.js code string for each
518	            section that received a diagram. Empty dict on total failure
519	            or when the feature is disabled.
520	        """
521	        if not PG_SMART_ART_ENABLED:
522	            logger.info("[smart_art] Feature disabled (PG_SMART_ART_ENABLED=0)")
523	            return {}
524	
525	        if not sections:
526	            logger.warning("[smart_art] No sections provided, returning empty")
527	            return {}
528	
529	        if not llm_client:
530	            logger.error("[smart_art] No LLM client provided")
531	            return {}
532	
533	        effective_max = min(max_diagrams, PG_MAX_SMART_ART)
534	        logger.info(
535	            "[smart_art] Starting diagram generation for %d sections (max=%d)",
536	            len(sections),
537	            effective_max,
538	        )
539	
540	        # Build evidence lookup: evidence_id -> statement
541	        evidence_by_id: dict[str, str] = {}
542	        for piece in evidence:
543	            eid = piece.get("evidence_id", "")
544	            statement = piece.get("statement", "")
545	            if eid and statement:
546	                evidence_by_id[eid] = statement
547	
548	        # Step 1: Analyze sections to identify diagram candidates
549	        recommendations = await self.analyze_sections(sections, llm_client)
550	
551	        if not recommendations:
552	            logger.info("[smart_art] No sections recommended for diagrams")
553	            return {}
554	
555	        # Enforce caller-specified cap
556	        if len(recommendations) > effective_max:
557	            recommendations = recommendations[:effective_max]
558	
559	        # Build section lookup for content retrieval
560	        section_by_id: dict[str, dict] = {}
561	        for sec in sections:
562	            sid = sec.get("section_id") or sec.get("id", "")
563	            if sid:
564	                section_by_id[sid] = sec
565	
566	        # Step 2: Generate Mermaid diagrams for each recommended section
567	        results: dict[str, str] = {}
568	        for rec in recommendations:
569	            sid = rec["section_id"]
570	            sec_data = section_by_id.get(sid)
571	            if not sec_data:
572	                logger.warning(
573	                    "[smart_art] Section '%s' not found in section lookup, skipping",
574	                    sid,
575	                )
576	                continue
577	
578	            section_content = sec_data.get("content", "")
579	            section_title = rec["section_title"]
580	            diagram_type = rec["diagram_type"]
581	
582	            # FIX-CITE-3/A3: Heuristic override for diagram type based on content
583	            table_count = section_content.count("|:---")
584	            comparison_words = sum(1 for w in ["versus", "vs.", "compared to", "in contrast"]
585	                                  if w in section_content.lower())
586	            if table_count >= 2 or comparison_words >= 3:
587	                if diagram_type not in ("comparison_matrix",):
588	                    diagram_type = "comparison_matrix"
589	                    logger.info(
590	                        "[smart_art] FIX-A3: Overrode type to comparison_matrix "
591	                        "for '%s' (tables=%d, comparisons=%d)",
592	                        section_title[:40], table_count, comparison_words,
593	                    )
594	
595	            # Gather evidence summaries for this section
596	            section_evidence_ids = sec_data.get("evidence_ids", [])
597	            evidence_summaries = [
598	                evidence_by_id[eid]
599	                for eid in section_evidence_ids
600	                if eid in evidence_by_id
601	            ]
602	
603	            mermaid_code = await self.generate_mermaid(
604	                section_title=section_title,
605	                section_content=section_content,
606	                evidence_summaries=evidence_summaries,
607	                diagram_type=diagram_type,
608	                llm_client=llm_client,
609	            )
610	
611	            if mermaid_code:
612	                results[sid] = mermaid_code
613	                if self._tracer:
614	                    self._tracer.log_event(
615	                        event_type="smart_art_generated",
616	                        data={
617	                            "section_id": sid,
618	                            "section_title": section_title,
619	                            "diagram_type": diagram_type,
620	                            "mermaid_length": len(mermaid_code),
621	                        },
622	                    )
623	            else:
624	                logger.warning(
625	                    "[smart_art] Failed to generate diagram for section '%s' (%s)",
626	                    sid,
627	                    diagram_type,
628	                )
629	
630	        logger.info(
631	            "[smart_art] Diagram generation complete: %d/%d successful",
632	            len(results),
633	            len(recommendations),
634	        )
635	        return results
636	
637	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
638	    # Internal helpers
639	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
640	
641	    @staticmethod
642	    def _format_sections_for_analysis(sections: list[dict]) -> str:
643	        """
644	        Format report sections into a compact text block for the analysis
645	        prompt. Includes section_id, title, and a truncated content preview
646	        to stay within token limits while providing enough context for the
647	        LLM to make informed diagram-type decisions.
648	        """
649	        max_content_preview: int = int(
650	            os.getenv("PG_SMART_ART_CONTENT_PREVIEW_CHARS", "1500")
651	        )
652	        parts: list[str] = []
653	        for sec in sections:
654	            sid = sec.get("section_id") or sec.get("id", "unknown")
655	            title = sec.get("title", "Untitled")
656	            content = sec.get("content", "")
657	            word_count = len(content.split())
658	            preview = content[:max_content_preview]
659	            if len(content) > max_content_preview:
660	                preview += " [...]"
661	            parts.append(
662	                f"--- Section {sid}: {title} ({word_count} words) ---\n{preview}"
663	            )
664	        return "\n\n".join(parts)
665

content.text: This file uses "SmartArt" — it's actually a genuine, verified diagram generator producing real Mermaid diagrams, so this is a legitimate domain term (matches the KEEP-BUT-NOTED example in the instructions). Clean file. Now let's read source_necessity.py.

content.content:
1	"""T3 (I-deepfix-001 #1344) — Source-necessity min-vertex-cover (Hopcroft-Karp), NEW module.
2	
3	DeepTRACE metric VI (Source Necessity). A report that LISTS many sources but whose factual claims
4	are all carried by a small load-bearing subset scores badly on #6: the listed references read as
5	padding. This module computes the necessity of each LISTED source FAITHFULLY — as the paper does —
6	by building the (statement, source) factual-support bipartite graph and computing its
7	**minimum vertex cover via Hopcroft-Karp maximum matching + König's construction**.
8	
9	WHAT IT PRODUCES (all DISCLOSURE / render-structure — the faithfulness engine is UNTOUCHED):
10	
11	  * ``necessity_ratio`` = necessary_sources / listed_sources (DeepTRACE #6), where a source is
12	    NECESSARY iff it is the SOLE supporter of at least one relevant, supported statement — the
13	    sources present in EVERY minimum vertex cover. This equals the sole-supporter reading used by
14	    the WS-14 benchmark scorer, so the number POLARIS discloses matches the number the benchmark
15	    measures.
16	  * ``min_vertex_cover`` — the actual minimum set of sources (+ statements) covering every support
17	    edge, via Hopcroft-Karp + König. Exposed for audit; every source in it is load-bearing.
18	  * ``zero_support`` — the listed sources that support NO relevant statement (genuine padding).
19	
20	§-1.3 DNA — WEIGHT-and-CONSOLIDATE, never FILTER-and-DROP:
21	
22	  * Necessity is computed over the support MATRIX, never the citation-layer render. Broad honest
23	    facet coverage (O1/F1) makes each source load-bearing for its OWN facet, so it lands in the
24	    min-cover — POLARIS raises #6 STRUCTURALLY, never by excluding honest corroborators.
25	  * A "redundant" (corroborated) source is KEPT at full weight — corroboration is the whole point
26	    of consolidate. This module NEVER drops it; it only reports the necessity split.
27	  * The ONE render move is QUARANTINE of ZERO-factual-support entries out of the LISTED reference
28	    set into a typed audit ledger: kept + disclosed, NEVER removed from the corpus. A zero-support
29	    source supports no statement, so it can never be a sole-supporter (necessary) — quarantining it
30	    can only RAISE the honest necessity ratio without touching any load-bearing source.
31	
32	The faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role D8 / provenance / span-grounding) runs
33	UPSTREAM and is not imported here. This module reads only the already-verified support structure.
34	PURE / offline. LAW VI kill-switch. snake_case.
35	"""
36	from __future__ import annotations
37	
38	import os
39	import re
40	from collections import deque
41	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
42	from typing import Any, Mapping, Optional, Sequence
43	
44	_QUARANTINE_ENV = "PG_SOURCE_NECESSITY_QUARANTINE"
45	_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"", "0", "false", "off", "no"})
46	
47	_LEDGER_HEADER = "## Source-necessity audit ledger (listed but non-load-bearing — not dropped)"
48	_BIB_ENTRY_LINE_RE = re.compile(r"^\[(\d+)\]\s")
49	
50	
51	def quarantine_enabled() -> bool:
52	    """T3 kill-switch. Default ON; OFF => no quarantine + no disclosure (byte-identical revert)."""
53	    return os.environ.get(_QUARANTINE_ENV, "1").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
54	
55	
56	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
57	# Hopcroft-Karp maximum bipartite matching + König minimum vertex cover
58	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
59	def hopcroft_karp_matching(
60	    adjacency: Mapping[Any, Sequence[Any]],
61	    left_nodes: Sequence[Any],
62	) -> dict[Any, Any]:
63	    """Maximum-cardinality matching of a bipartite graph via Hopcroft-Karp.
64	
65	    ``adjacency`` maps each LEFT node to the RIGHT nodes it is joined to; ``left_nodes`` is the
66	    (deterministically ordered) left vertex set. Returns ``match_right`` mapping each MATCHED right
67	    node to its left partner. PURE — O(E * sqrt(V)). Deterministic for a fixed input ordering.
68	    """
69	    match_left: dict[Any, Any] = {}
70	    match_right: dict[Any, Any] = {}
71	    inf = float("inf")
72	
73	    def bfs() -> bool:
74	        dist: dict[Any, float] = {}
75	        queue: deque = deque()
76	        for u in left_nodes:
77	            if u not in match_left:
78	                dist[u] = 0
79	                queue.append(u)
80	            else:
81	                dist[u] = inf
82	        found = False
83	        while queue:
84	            u = queue.popleft()
85	            for v in adjacency.get(u, ()):  # right neighbours
86	                w = match_right.get(v)  # left node currently matched to v (or None)
87	                if w is None:
88	                    found = True
89	                elif dist.get(w, inf) == inf:
90	                    dist[w] = dist[u] + 1
91	                    queue.append(w)
92	        return found
93	
94	    def dfs(u: Any, dist: dict[Any, float]) -> bool:
95	        for v in adjacency.get(u, ()):
96	            w = match_right.get(v)
97	            if w is None or (dist.get(w, float("inf")) == dist[u] + 1 and dfs(w, dist)):
98	                match_left[u] = v
99	                match_right[v] = u
100	                return True
101	        dist[u] = float("inf")
102	        return False
103	
104	    # Hopcroft-Karp phases: recompute layered distances, then augment along shortest paths.
105	    while True:
106	        dist: dict[Any, float] = {}
107	        queue = deque()
108	        for u in left_nodes:
109	            if u not in match_left:
110	                dist[u] = 0
111	                queue.append(u)
112	            else:
113	                dist[u] = inf
114	        reached_free = False
115	        while queue:
116	            u = queue.popleft()
117	            for v in adjacency.get(u, ()):
118	                w = match_right.get(v)
119	                if w is None:
120	                    reached_free = True
121	                elif dist.get(w, inf) == inf:
122	                    dist[w] = dist[u] + 1
123	                    queue.append(w)
124	        if not reached_free:
125	            break
126	        for u in left_nodes:
127	            if u not in match_left:
128	                dfs(u, dist)
129	    return dict(match_right)
130	
131	
132	def min_vertex_cover(
133	    left_nodes: Sequence[Any],
134	    right_nodes: Sequence[Any],
135	    adjacency: Mapping[Any, Sequence[Any]],
136	) -> tuple[set[Any], set[Any]]:
137	    """Minimum vertex cover of a bipartite graph (König's theorem construction).
138	
139	    Returns ``(cover_left, cover_right)``. |cover| == size of the maximum matching. Every support
140	    edge has an endpoint in the cover. PURE / deterministic. Used to expose the exact load-bearing
141	    source set; sole-supporter sources are a subset of ``cover_right`` (they are forced).
142	    """
143	    left_nodes = list(left_nodes)
144	    right_nodes = list(right_nodes)
145	    match_right = hopcroft_karp_matching(adjacency, left_nodes)
146	    matched_left = set(match_right.values())
147	
148	    # König: start Z from unmatched LEFT nodes; alternate NOT-in-matching (L->R) and
149	    # in-matching (R->L) edges. Min cover = (L \ Z) ∪ (R ∩ Z).
150	    match_left = {v: u for u, v in match_right.items()}  # left -> right
151	    visited_left: set[Any] = set()
152	    visited_right: set[Any] = set()
153	    stack: list[Any] = [u for u in left_nodes if u not in matched_left]
154	    for u in stack:
155	        visited_left.add(u)
156	    work = deque(stack)
157	    while work:
158	        u = work.popleft()
159	        for v in adjacency.get(u, ()):
160	            # traverse only NON-matching edges L->R
161	            if match_left.get(u) == v:
162	                continue
163	            if v not in visited_right:
164	                visited_right.add(v)
165	                w = match_right.get(v)  # its matched left partner
166	                if w is not None and w not in visited_left:
167	                    visited_left.add(w)
168	                    work.append(w)
169	    cover_left = {u for u in left_nodes if u not in visited_left}
170	    cover_right = {v for v in right_nodes if v in visited_right}
171	    return cover_left, cover_right
172	
173	
174	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
175	# Source-necessity over the LISTED-source support graph
176	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
177	@dataclass
178	class SourceNecessity:
179	    """The necessity split over the listed-source support graph. All fields are DISCLOSURE."""
180	
181	    listed_sources: int
182	    necessary_sources: int
183	    necessity_ratio: float
184	    necessary_ids: tuple = field(default_factory=tuple)   # sole-supporter (forced) source ids
185	    redundant_ids: tuple = field(default_factory=tuple)   # supporting but corroborated (kept)
186	    zero_support_ids: tuple = field(default_factory=tuple)  # support no statement (quarantine target)
187	    min_cover_size: int = 0
188	
189	
190	def compute_source_necessity(
191	    support_by_source: Mapping[Any, Sequence[Any]],
192	    listed_sources: Sequence[Any],
193	    d8_verified_cited_nums: "Sequence[Any] | frozenset | set | None" = None,
194	) -> SourceNecessity:
195	    """Compute DeepTRACE #6 source-necessity over the LISTED-source universe.
196	
197	    ``support_by_source`` maps a source id -> the statement ids whose OWN isolated span it SUPPORTS
198	    (the same span-verified members counted in ``verified_support_origin_count``; never the advisory
199	    clustered count). ``listed_sources`` is the full listed reference set (the same universe #8
200	    thoroughness uses). A source absent from ``support_by_source`` (or mapped to []) supports no
201	    statement -> ``zero_support``. A source that is the SOLE supporter of >=1 statement is
202	    NECESSARY (present in every minimum vertex cover). Everything else that supports >=1 statement
203	    is REDUNDANT (corroborated — KEPT at full weight, never dropped).
204	
205	    I-deepfix-001 tail-B1 (#1344, finding #7 — necessity/D8 reconciliation): ``d8_verified_cited_nums``
206	    is the set of LISTED sources that are cited in the body AND carry a VERIFIED four-role D8 settled
207	    verdict. The isolated-span basket layer (``support_by_source``) can DISAGREE with the D8 settled
208	    verdict — a body-cited source can be D8-VERIFIED yet have an isolated-span basket that is span-
209	    unverified, so it lands in ``support_by_source`` with NO statement. Per CLAUSE §-1.3 the four-role
210	    D8 engine is the ONE hard gate, so such a source IS load-bearing: it is credited as NECESSARY here
211	    (it is the D8-verified support for a rendered claim) and can therefore NEVER be reported as
212	    zero-support. This STRENGTHENS the necessity reading (a genuinely-load-bearing source counts); it
213	    never relaxes any verification. Empty/None -> byte-identical to the pre-fix behaviour.
214	
215	    PURE. Never mutates inputs. Faithfulness-neutral (reads already-verified support only).
216	    """
217	    listed = [str(s) for s in listed_sources]
218	    listed_set = set(listed)
219	    n = len(listed)
220	    # The D8-VERIFIED body-cited allowlist, bounded to the listed universe (a num absent from the
221	    # listed set is irrelevant here). Compared as strings so an int-keyed caller and a str-keyed
222	    # support map reconcile on the SAME identity.
223	    d8_allow = {str(x) for x in (d8_verified_cited_nums or ())} & listed_set
224	
225	    # Build statement -> supporting listed sources (bounded to the listed universe).
226	    supporters_by_statement: dict[Any, set[str]] = {}
227	    supported_listed: set[str] = set()
228	    for src, stmts in (support_by_source or {}).items():
229	        sid = str(src)
230	        if sid not in listed_set:
231	            continue
232	        for st in stmts or ():
233	            supporters_by_statement.setdefault(str(st), set()).add(sid)
234	            supported_listed.add(sid)
235	
236	    # Sole-supporter (necessary) sources = present in EVERY minimum cover.
237	    necessary: set[str] = set()
238	    for _st, sups in supporters_by_statement.items():
239	        if len(sups) == 1:
240	            necessary.add(next(iter(sups)))
241	
242	    # D8 reconciliation: a body-cited D8-VERIFIED source with NO isolated-span support is STILL
243	    # load-bearing (D8 settled its rendered claim VERIFIED). Credit it as NECESSARY and mark it
244	    # supported, so it can never be reported as zero-support and it counts toward the necessity ratio.
245	    for sid in d8_allow:
246	        if sid not in supported_listed:
247	            necessary.add(sid)
248	            supported_listed.add(sid)
249	
250	    zero_support = sorted(listed_set - supported_listed)
251	    redundant = sorted(supported_listed - necessary)
252	
253	    # Faithful minimum vertex cover via Hopcroft-Karp + König over the support edges (audit).
254	    statement_ids = list(supporters_by_statement.keys())
255	    adjacency: dict[Any, list[str]] = {}
256	    for st, sups in supporters_by_statement.items():
257	        adjacency[("stmt", st)] = [("src", s) for s in sorted(sups)]
258	    left = [("stmt", st) for st in statement_ids]
259	    right = [("src", s) for s in sorted(supported_listed)]
260	    cover_left, cover_right = min_vertex_cover(left, right, adjacency)
261	    min_cover_size = len(cover_left) + len(cover_right)
262	
263	    ratio = (len(necessary) / n) if n else 0.0
264	    return SourceNecessity(
265	        listed_sources=n,
266	        necessary_sources=len(necessary),
267	        necessity_ratio=round(ratio, 4),
268	        necessary_ids=tuple(sorted(necessary)),
269	        redundant_ids=tuple(redundant),
270	        zero_support_ids=tuple(zero_support),
271	        min_cover_size=min_cover_size,
272	    )
273	
274	
275	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
276	# Render transform: quarantine zero-support cited entries + disclose necessity
277	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
278	def _necessity_disclosure_line(necessity: SourceNecessity) -> str:
279	    return (
280	        "_Source necessity (DeepTRACE metric VI, minimum-vertex-cover reading): "
281	        f"{necessity.necessary_sources} of {necessity.listed_sources} listed references are "
282	        "NECESSARY (each is the sole supporter of at least one report claim); necessity ratio "
283	        f"{necessity.necessity_ratio:.4f}. Non-load-bearing listed entries are moved to the audit "
284	        "ledger below — kept and disclosed, never dropped from the corpus (WEIGHT-and-CONSOLIDATE)._"
285	    )
286	
287	
288	def retype_bibliography_by_source_necessity(
289	    biblio_section_text: str,
290	    zero_support_nums: set[int],
291	    necessity: SourceNecessity,
292	    d8_verified_cited_nums: "Sequence[int] | frozenset | set | None" = None,
293	    body_cited_nums: "Sequence[int] | frozenset | set | None" = None,
294	) -> str:
295	    """Move zero-factual-support cited entry lines out of "## Bibliography" into a typed
296	    source-necessity audit ledger, and append the necessity disclosure. PURE string surgery.
297	
298	    An entry line (``^[N] ...``) whose N is in ``zero_support_nums`` moves VERBATIM under the
299	    ledger header; every other line stays. Nothing is re-rendered, invented, or deleted — a
300	    quarantined source still ships in report.md, typed as a non-load-bearing audit row. Returns the
301	    input UNCHANGED when there is nothing to quarantine (byte-identical) so the caller can no-op.
302	
303	    I-deepfix-001 tail-B1 (#1344, finding #7): ``d8_verified_cited_nums`` is a DEFENSIVE allowlist —
304	    a number carrying a VERIFIED four-role D8 settled verdict is NEVER moved to the ledger even if it
305	    was (wrongly) passed in ``zero_support_nums``, so a D8-VERIFIED body-cited source keeps its
306	    numbered bibliography entry and its in-text ``[N]`` marker never dangles. Normally the caller has
307	    already excluded the allowlist upstream (``zero_support_bib_nums``); this is the belt-and-braces
308	    guard at the render seam.
309	
310	    I-wire-001 F1 (dangling-citation prune): ``body_cited_nums`` is the same DEFENSIVE guard, widened
311	    to EVERY number whose ``[N]`` marker appears in the rendered body — never move a bibliography entry
312	    the body still cites (its marker would then dangle, the drb_72 [85]/[104]/[110] defect). Empty/None
313	    for either allowlist -> byte-identical to the pre-fix behaviour.
314	    """
315	    if not biblio_section_text:
316	        return biblio_section_text
317	    # DEFENSIVE (finding #7 + F1): never quarantine a D8-VERIFIED number OR any body-cited number,
318	    # even if a stale caller left it in `zero_support_nums` (a body-cited entry must stay resolvable).
319	    allow = {int(n) for n in (d8_verified_cited_nums or ())}
320	    allow |= {int(n) for n in (body_cited_nums or ())}
321	    effective_zero = {int(n) for n in (zero_support_nums or set())} - allow
322	    if not effective_zero:
323	        # Still surface the necessity number when there ARE listed sources; but keep byte-identity
324	        # when the disclosure would be vacuous (no listed sources).
325	        if necessity.listed_sources <= 0:
326	            return biblio_section_text
327	    lines = biblio_section_text.split("\n")
328	    kept: list[str] = []
329	    ledger: list[str] = []
330	    for line in lines:
331	        m = _BIB_ENTRY_LINE_RE.match(line)
332	        if m and int(m.group(1)) in effective_zero:
333	            ledger.append(line)
334	        else:
335	            kept.append(line)
336	    kept_block = "\n".join(kept).rstrip()
337	    disclosure = _necessity_disclosure_line(necessity)
338	    if not ledger:
339	        # No quarantine, but disclose the necessity number inline (kept references only).
340	        return kept_block + "\n\n" + disclosure + "\n"
341	    ledger_block = "\n".join(ledger).strip()
342	    return (
343	        kept_block
344	        + "\n\n"
345	        + disclosure
346	        + "\n\n"
347	        + _LEDGER_HEADER
348	        + "\n\n"
349	        + "_These listed sources support no report claim under isolated span verification. They are "
350	        "disclosed here for audit completeness and remain in the corpus at full weight; they are not "
351	        "part of the load-bearing cited reference set._\n\n"
352	        + ledger_block
353	        + "\n"
354	    )
355	
356	
357	def zero_support_bib_nums(
358	    support_by_num: Mapping[int, Sequence[Any]],
359	    cited_nums: Sequence[int],
360	    d8_verified_cited_nums: "Sequence[int] | frozenset | set | None" = None,
361	    body_cited_nums: "Sequence[int] | frozenset | set | None" = None,
362	) -> set[int]:
363	    """The cited bibliography numbers that support NO statement (quarantine targets). PURE.
364	
365	    ``support_by_num`` maps a bibliography number -> the statement ids it span-verified SUPPORTS.
366	    ``cited_nums`` is the set of numbers actually cited in the body. A cited number with an empty
367	    (or missing) support list is zero-support. Only CITED numbers are eligible (an uncited row is
368	    already handled by the T2 corpus-ledger typing).
369	
370	    I-deepfix-001 tail-B1 (#1344, finding #7): ``d8_verified_cited_nums`` is the allowlist of
371	    body-cited numbers that carry a VERIFIED four-role D8 settled verdict. Such a number is
372	    load-bearing under the ONE hard gate (§-1.3), so it is NEVER a quarantine target even when its
373	    isolated-span basket is span-unverified — this prevents a D8-VERIFIED cited source from being
374	    quarantined out of the numbered bibliography (which would leave its in-text ``[N]`` marker
375	    dangling). Empty/None -> byte-identical to the pre-fix behaviour.
376	
377	    I-wire-001 F1 (dangling-citation prune): ``body_cited_nums`` is the set of numbers whose ``[N]``
378	    marker appears in the RENDERED BODY (sections / key findings / summary table / disclosures).
379	    ANY such number is protected from quarantine — a marker the reader can follow in the body MUST
380	    resolve to a listed reference, so moving its entry into the audit ledger would dangle the
381	    citation (the drb_72 defect where the body cites [85]/[104]/[110] but the bibliography skips
382	    them). This is the general form of the D8 allowlist: it protects EVERY body-cited number, not
383	    only the D8-verified subset. Empty/None -> byte-identical to the pre-fix behaviour."""
384	    cited = {int(n) for n in cited_nums}
385	    allow = {int(n) for n in (d8_verified_cited_nums or ())}
386	    body_cited = {int(n) for n in (body_cited_nums or ())}
387	    out: set[int] = set()
388	    for n in cited:
389	        if n in allow:
390	            continue  # D8-VERIFIED cited source is load-bearing; never quarantine (finding #7).
391	        if n in body_cited:
392	            continue  # its [N] appears in the rendered body; never dangle the citation (F1).
393	        stmts = support_by_num.get(n)
394	        if not stmts:
395	            out.add(n)
396	    return out
397

content.content:
1	"""I-deepfix-006-compose C1 — the ADDITIVE entailment verify path for cross-source SYNTHESIS.
2	
3	WHY: ``depth_synthesis.synthesize_cross_source_findings`` re-grounds every synthesized cross-source
4	sentence through the FROZEN ``strict_verify`` engine, whose faithfulness floor is (a) a resolvable
5	provenance token, (b) every number in the sentence present in the cited span, and (c) a >=2 VERBATIM
6	content-word overlap between the sentence and the span. Leg (c) is LEXICAL: a genuine PARAPHRASE that
7	consolidates several corroborating spans into one clean news-style sentence (the whole point of the
8	synthesis pass) shares fewer than two verbatim content words with any single span and is DROPPED —
9	even though it is a faithful, number-matched, ENTAILED restatement of the evidence. That is the ~11
10	eligible baskets going 3->0 in the depth forensic.
11	
12	THE FIX (this module, default-ON ``PG_SYNTH_ENTAILMENT_VERIFY``): an ADDITIVE second verify leg that
13	keeps a synthesized sentence iff (a) it resolves >=1 provenance token to a cited span in the SAME
14	basket-scoped pool, (b) every number in it appears in that span (the SAME numeric leg strict_verify
15	uses — the module-level ``_decimals_in`` / ``_numbers_in`` helpers, reused UNCHANGED), and (c) the
16	cited span ENTAILS the sentence via the existing directional NLI judge
17	(``consolidation_nli.entails_directional``) — IN PLACE of the >=2-verbatim-content-word test.
18	
19	FAITHFULNESS (the frozen engine is UNTOUCHED — nothing here relaxes it):
20	  * ``strict_verify`` itself is never edited. This is a SEPARATE verify_fn whose kept sentences are
21	    UNIONED with strict_verify's (see ``make_entailment_union_verify_fn``) — strict_verify passes are
22	    ALWAYS kept; the entailment leg only ADDS entailed paraphrases the lexical leg dropped. It can
23	    never REMOVE a strict_verify pass, so the union is a pure superset (ADDITIVE, never a regression).
24	  * A paraphrase that MOVES / mangles a number fails the numeric leg and is DROPPED — the numeric
25	    invariant #3 is byte-for-byte the same predicate the frozen engine applies.
26	  * A NON-entailed paraphrase (the judge returns False) is DROPPED. Only a directionally-ENTAILED
27	    span->sentence relation is kept — the ALCE / DeepTRACE citation direction (span entails claim).
28	  * On a judge DEGRADE (the cross-encoder is unavailable / faulted => ``None``) we do NOT fight
29	    breadth on an infra fault: we fall back to the SAME strict >=2 verbatim content-word overlap the
30	    frozen engine uses, so a degrade can never keep an ungrounded paraphrase.
31	
32	The default entailment judge is the SAME resident cross-encoder the consolidation leg already loads
33	(``entails_directional``, lazy) — ZERO new model / OpenRouter spend. Tests inject a deterministic
34	``entails_fn`` so the whole path is proven OFFLINE with no GPU / network.
35	"""
36	from __future__ import annotations
37	
38	import logging
39	import os
40	import re
41	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
42	from typing import Any, Callable, Optional
43	
44	from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import (
45	    _INTEGER_PERCENT_RE,
46	    _PLACEBO_COMPARATOR_RE,
47	    _THRESHOLD_RE,
48	    ProvenanceToken,
49	    SentenceVerification,
50	    _content_words,
51	    _decimals_in,
52	    _numbers_in,
53	    _strip_dose_patterns,
54	    _verifier_cleaned_text,
55	    parse_provenance_tokens,
56	    split_into_sentences,
57	)
58	
59	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
60	
61	# Default-ON master gate (LAW VI). OFF => the union wrapper is never applied and the synthesis pass
62	# re-grounds through the FROZEN strict_verify ALONE (byte-identical to pre-C1).
63	_ENV_ENTAILMENT_VERIFY = "PG_SYNTH_ENTAILMENT_VERIFY"
64	
65	# The degrade fallback overlap floor — the SAME >=2 the frozen engine's content-word leg uses.
66	_MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP_DEGRADE = 2
67	
68	# A resolved provenance token, for the dedup key of the union wrapper.
69	_EV_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\[#ev:[A-Za-z0-9_]+:\d+-\d+\]")
70	
71	# The soft-warning that marks a sentence KEPT by the entailment leg (read by the C3 D8-promote routing).
72	SYNTH_ENTAILMENT_SOFT_WARNING = "synthesis_entailment_verified"
73	
74	# entails_fn(premise, hypothesis) -> True (entails) | False (does not) | None (judge unavailable / degrade)
75	EntailsFn = Callable[[str, str], Optional[bool]]
76	
77	
78	def entailment_verify_enabled() -> bool:
79	    """Default-ON. OFF (``PG_SYNTH_ENTAILMENT_VERIFY=0``) => the entailment union is never wired and the
80	    synthesis pass re-grounds through the frozen ``strict_verify`` alone (byte-identical to pre-C1)."""
81	    return os.getenv(_ENV_ENTAILMENT_VERIFY, "1").strip().lower() not in ("", "0", "false", "off", "no")
82	
83	
84	def _default_entails_fn(premise: str, hypothesis: str) -> Optional[bool]:
85	    """The production entailment judge: the SAME resident cross-encoder the consolidation leg loads
86	    (``consolidation_nli.entails_directional`` — span=premise, claim=hypothesis). Lazy import so this
87	    module stays cheap. Any import/scoring fault surfaces as ``None`` (=> the DEGRADE fallback), never a
88	    crash and never a silent True."""
89	    try:
90	        from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.consolidation_nli import entails_directional
91	    except Exception as exc:  # pragma: no cover — consolidation_nli is stable in-tree
92	        logger.warning("[synthesis_entailment_verify] entails_directional unavailable (%s) => degrade", exc)
93	        return None
94	    try:
95	        return entails_directional(premise, hypothesis)
96	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — an infra fault degrades, never crashes the verify
97	        logger.warning("[synthesis_entailment_verify] entails_directional raised (%s) => degrade", exc)
98	        return None
99	
100	
101	@dataclass
102	class _EntailmentVerificationReport:
103	    """A minimal report exposing the ONE attribute the depth synthesis ``_collect`` reads: a list of
104	    kept ``SentenceVerification`` (each carrying ``.sentence`` with its [#ev:...] tokens and ``.tokens``)."""
105	
106	    kept_sentences: list[SentenceVerification] = field(default_factory=list)
107	
108	
109	def _resolve_spans(
110	    tokens: list[ProvenanceToken], evidence_pool: dict
111	) -> "tuple[list[ProvenanceToken], list[str]]":
112	    """Resolve each token to its cited span text in the BASKET-SCOPED pool (a token whose evidence_id is
113	    absent from the scoped pool — a cross-basket citation — is skipped, so it fails CLOSED). Span bounds
114	    are validated with the SAME predicate the frozen engine uses (in-bounds, start < end)."""
115	    valid: list[ProvenanceToken] = []
116	    spans: list[str] = []
117	    for tok in tokens:
118	        ev = (evidence_pool or {}).get(tok.evidence_id)
119	        if ev is None:
120	            continue
121	        direct_quote = ev.get("direct_quote") or ev.get("statement") or ""
122	        if tok.end > len(direct_quote) or tok.start < 0 or tok.start >= tok.end:
123	            continue
124	        span_text = direct_quote[tok.start:tok.end]
125	        if not span_text.strip():
126	            continue
127	        valid.append(tok)
128	        spans.append(span_text)
129	    return valid, spans
130	
131	
132	def _numbers_match(sentence_core: str, span_texts: list[str]) -> bool:
133	    """Leg (b): the SAME numeric leg strict_verify applies (invariant #3), reused UNCHANGED via the
134	    module-level helpers. Every decimal in the sentence must appear in the union of the cited-span
135	    decimals; when the sentence carries no decimals, every standalone integer must appear in the union of
136	    the cited-span numbers. Placebo-comparator + achievement-threshold numbers are stripped exactly as
137	    the frozen engine strips them, and a %-expressed integer beside a decimal is also required in-span."""
138	    stripped = _strip_dose_patterns(sentence_core)
139	    stripped = _PLACEBO_COMPARATOR_RE.sub(" ", stripped)
140	    stripped = _THRESHOLD_RE.sub(" ", stripped)
141	
142	    agg_decimals: set[str] = set()
143	    agg_numbers: set[str] = set()
144	    for span in span_texts:
145	        span_stripped = _strip_dose_patterns(span)
146	        agg_decimals |= _decimals_in(span_stripped)
147	        agg_numbers |= _numbers_in(span_stripped)
148	
149	    sentence_decimals = _decimals_in(stripped)
150	    if sentence_decimals:
151	        if sentence_decimals - agg_decimals:
152	            return False
153	        claimed_pct_ints = {
154	            m.group(1) for m in _INTEGER_PERCENT_RE.finditer(stripped)
155	        } - sentence_decimals
156	        if claimed_pct_ints:
157	            agg_int_only = agg_numbers - agg_decimals
158	            if claimed_pct_ints - agg_int_only:
159	                return False
160	        return True
161	
162	    sentence_numbers = _numbers_in(stripped)
163	    if sentence_numbers and (sentence_numbers - agg_numbers):
164	        return False
165	    return True
166	
167	
168	def _entails_or_degrade(premise: str, hypothesis: str, entails_fn: EntailsFn) -> bool:
169	    """Leg (c): the span (premise) must ENTAIL the sentence (hypothesis). True => keep; False => drop.
170	    ``None`` (the judge is unavailable / degraded) => do NOT fight breadth on an infra fault: fall back
171	    to the SAME strict >=2 verbatim content-word overlap the frozen engine uses (so a degrade can never
172	    keep an ungrounded paraphrase)."""
173	    verdict = entails_fn(premise, hypothesis)
174	    if verdict is True:
175	        return True
176	    if verdict is False:
177	        return False
178	    return len(_content_words(hypothesis) & _content_words(premise)) >= _MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP_DEGRADE
179	
180	
181	def entailment_verify(
182	    draft_text: str, evidence_pool: dict, *, entails_fn: Optional[EntailsFn] = None
183	) -> _EntailmentVerificationReport:
184	    """Verify a synthesis draft through the ADDITIVE entailment leg. Keeps a sentence iff (a) it resolves
185	    >=1 provenance token to a cited span, (b) its numbers match the span (frozen numeric leg), and (c)
186	    the span ENTAILS the sentence. Returns a report whose ``kept_sentences`` mirror strict_verify's
187	    (each carries ``.sentence`` with its [#ev:...] tokens and ``.tokens``)."""
188	    entails_fn = _default_entails_fn if entails_fn is None else entails_fn
189	    kept: list[SentenceVerification] = []
190	    for raw_sentence in split_into_sentences(draft_text or ""):
191	        sentence = raw_sentence.strip()
192	        if not sentence:
193	            continue
194	        tokens = parse_provenance_tokens(sentence)
195	        if not tokens:
196	            continue
197	        valid_tokens, span_texts = _resolve_spans(tokens, evidence_pool)
198	        if not valid_tokens or not span_texts:
199	            continue
200	        core = _verifier_cleaned_text(sentence)  # strip [#ev:] / atom artifacts for the numeric + NLI input
201	        if not core.strip():
202	            continue
203	        if not _numbers_match(core, span_texts):
204	            continue
205	        premise = " ".join(span_texts)
206	        if not _entails_or_degrade(premise, core, entails_fn):
207	            continue
208	        kept.append(
209	            SentenceVerification(
210	                sentence=sentence,
211	                tokens=valid_tokens,
212	                is_verified=True,
213	                soft_warnings=[SYNTH_ENTAILMENT_SOFT_WARNING],
214	            )
215	        )
216	    return _EntailmentVerificationReport(kept_sentences=kept)
217	
218	
219	def entailment_grounds_sentence(
220	    sentence: str, cited_rows: list, *, entails_fn: Optional[EntailsFn] = None
221	) -> bool:
222	    """The C3 promote confirmation (the ENTAILMENT analog of the D3 ``_frozen_engine_verifies_sentence``
223	    hook): reconstruct the cited spans from ``cited_rows`` (each row's full ``direct_quote`` / ``statement``)
224	    and confirm the synthesis sentence is number-grounded in the union AND ENTAILED by it. True => the
225	    sentence may be PROMOTED into the D8 4-role input set; a non-entailed / number-mismatched sentence =>
226	    False (not promoted). FAIL-CLOSED on no spans / empty prose."""
227	    entails_fn = _default_entails_fn if entails_fn is None else entails_fn
228	    core = _verifier_cleaned_text(str(sentence or ""))
229	    if not core.strip():
230	        return False
231	    span_texts: list[str] = []
232	    for row in (cited_rows or []):
233	        if not hasattr(row, "get"):  # accept any Mapping (dict / evidence record), skip non-mappings
234	            continue
235	        span = str(row.get("direct_quote") or row.get("statement") or "")
236	        if span.strip():
237	            span_texts.append(span)
238	    if not span_texts:
239	        return False
240	    if not _numbers_match(core, span_texts):
241	        return False
242	    premise = " ".join(span_texts)
243	    return _entails_or_degrade(premise, core, entails_fn)
244	
245	
246	def _norm_key(sentence: str) -> str:
247	    """Dedup key for the union: strip [#ev:...] tokens, collapse whitespace, lowercase."""
248	    bare = _EV_TOKEN_RE.sub(" ", sentence or "")
249	    return re.sub(r"\s+", " ", bare).strip().lower()
250	
251	
252	def make_entailment_union_verify_fn(
253	    base_verify_fn: Callable[..., Any], *, entails_fn: Optional[EntailsFn] = None
254	) -> Callable[..., Any]:
255	    """Wrap ``base_verify_fn`` (= the FROZEN ``strict_verify``) so its kept sentences are UNIONED with the
256	    entailment leg's kept sentences (dedup by normalized sentence). ADDITIVE: every strict_verify pass is
257	    kept unchanged; the entailment leg only ADDS entailed paraphrases the lexical >=2-content-word leg
258	    dropped. Each entailment-only addition carries the ``synthesis_entailment_verified`` soft-warning so
259	    the C3 D8-promote routing can recognize it. The returned callable has the SAME ``(draft, pool)``
260	    call shape ``synthesize_cross_source_findings`` invokes."""
261	    entails_fn = _default_entails_fn if entails_fn is None else entails_fn
262	
263	    def _wrapped(draft: Any, pool: dict, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> Any:
264	        base_report = base_verify_fn(draft, pool, *args, **kwargs)
265	        base_kept = list(getattr(base_report, "kept_sentences", None) or [])
266	        seen = {_norm_key(str(getattr(sv, "sentence", "") or "")) for sv in base_kept}
267	        seen.discard("")
268	        try:
269	            ent_report = entailment_verify(draft, pool, entails_fn=entails_fn)
270	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — the entailment leg is ADDITIVE; a fault keeps strict_verify only
271	            logger.warning("[synthesis_entailment_verify] entailment leg raised => strict_verify only", exc_info=True)
272	            return base_report
273	        merged = list(base_kept)
274	        for sv in ent_report.kept_sentences:
275	            key = _norm_key(str(getattr(sv, "sentence", "") or ""))
276	            if not key or key in seen:
277	                continue
278	            seen.add(key)
279	            if SYNTH_ENTAILMENT_SOFT_WARNING not in sv.soft_warnings:
280	                sv.soft_warnings.append(SYNTH_ENTAILMENT_SOFT_WARNING)
281	            merged.append(sv)
282	        try:
283	            base_report.kept_sentences = merged  # preserve every other field on the frozen report
284	            return base_report
285	        except Exception:  # pragma: no cover — StrictVerificationReport is a mutable dataclass
286	            return _EntailmentVerificationReport(kept_sentences=merged)
287	
288	    return _wrapped
289

content.text: Clean file — technical, professional naming throughout. Now synthesizer_v2.py.

content.content:
1	"""v2 Section Synthesizer (Parallel Writers with Fallbacks).
2	
3	Writes report sections in parallel using LangGraph Send API, with:
4	- TPM throttling via llm_throttle (Fix R5-#3)
5	- Phantom figure scrubbing (Fix R5-#5)
6	- Safe fallback on failure (Fix R6-#4)
7	- Evidence-grouped prompts (Fix R1-#4)
8	- Dynamic word targets (Fix R3-#5)
9	- Zero-evidence bypass (Fix R3-#2)
10	
11	Each section writer receives its SectionSpec from the Blueprint and
12	writes independently. Results merge via merge_sections_reducer (Fix R5-#1).
13	"""
14	
15	from __future__ import annotations
16	
17	import logging
18	import os
19	import re
20	from typing import Any
21	
22	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.citation_normalizer import normalize_citations
23	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.llm_throttle import throttled_llm_call
24	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.section_blueprint import (
25	    EMPTY_SECTION_PLACEHOLDER,
26	    SectionBlueprint,
27	    SectionSpec,
28	)
29	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.source_registry import SourceRegistry
30	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.synthesis_prompts import build_section_writer_prompt
31	from src.polaris_graph.state import ReportSection
32	
33	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph")
34	
35	# Max tokens per section generation call
36	MAX_SECTION_TOKENS = int(os.getenv("PG_V2_MAX_SECTION_TOKENS", "8192"))
37	
38	
39	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
40	# Public API
41	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
42	
43	async def write_section(
44	    client: Any,
45	    spec: SectionSpec,
46	    evidence_pool: list[dict[str, Any]],
47	    blueprint: SectionBlueprint,
48	    registry: SourceRegistry,
49	) -> dict[str, ReportSection]:
50	    """Write a single section from its Blueprint spec.
51	
52	    Fix R6-#4 (Parallel Fallback): This function is called via LangGraph's
53	    Send API in parallel for up to 15 sections. If ANY call throws an
54	    unhandled exception, asyncio.gather cancels ALL sibling tasks.
55	    The top-level try/except ensures a safe fallback is always returned.
56	
57	    Returns:
58	        dict[section_id, ReportSection] — for merge_sections_reducer.
59	    """
60	    try:
61	        return await _write_section_inner(
62	            client, spec, evidence_pool, blueprint, registry,
63	        )
64	    except Exception as e:
65	        logger.error(
66	            "Section '%s' failed, using fallback: %s",
67	            spec.title, str(e)[:200],
68	        )
69	        return {spec.section_id: _fallback_section(spec, str(e))}
70	
71	
72	async def _write_section_inner(
73	    client: Any,
74	    spec: SectionSpec,
75	    evidence_pool: list[dict[str, Any]],
76	    blueprint: SectionBlueprint,
77	    registry: SourceRegistry,
78	) -> dict[str, ReportSection]:
79	    """Core section writing logic (unwrapped for clean error propagation)."""
80	
81	    # Fix R3-#2: Zero-evidence bypass — emit placeholder, skip LLM entirely
82	    if spec.should_skip_llm:
83	        logger.info("Section '%s': 0 evidence, emitting placeholder", spec.title)
84	        return {spec.section_id: _placeholder_section(spec)}
85	
86	    # Retrieve assigned evidence
87	    evidence = blueprint.get_evidence_for_section(spec, evidence_pool)
88	    if not evidence:
89	        return {spec.section_id: _placeholder_section(spec)}
90	
91	    # Fix R1-#4: Group evidence by source to avoid repeating metadata
92	    grouped = SectionBlueprint.group_evidence_by_source(evidence)
93	
94	    # Build the evidence prompt
95	    evidence_prompt = _build_evidence_prompt(grouped, registry)
96	
97	    # Build system prompt with all rules
98	    system = build_section_writer_prompt(
99	        n_evidence=spec.evidence_count,
100	        suggested_words=spec.effective_target_words,
101	    )
102	
103	    # Build user prompt
104	    user_prompt = (
105	        f"## Section: {spec.title}\n\n"
106	        f"**Description:** {spec.description}\n\n"
107	        f"Write this section using ONLY the evidence below. "
108	        f"Cite sources using [SRC-NNN] format.\n\n"
109	        f"---\n\n{evidence_prompt}"
110	    )
111	
112	    # Fix R5-#3: Throttled LLM call (prevents TPM burst)
113	    max_tokens = min(
114	        MAX_SECTION_TOKENS,
115	        spec.effective_target_words * 3,  # ~3 tokens per word (generous)
116	    )
117	
118	    llm_response = await throttled_llm_call(
119	        client.generate,
120	        prompt=user_prompt,
121	        system=system,
122	        max_tokens=max_tokens,
123	    )
124	
125	    # Extract text from LLMResponse object
126	    raw_content = llm_response.content if hasattr(llm_response, "content") else str(llm_response)
127	
128	    if not raw_content or not raw_content.strip():
129	        logger.warning("Section '%s': LLM returned empty content", spec.title)
130	        return {spec.section_id: _placeholder_section(spec)}
131	
132	    # Fix R3-#3: Normalize compound citations
133	    content = normalize_citations(raw_content.strip())
134	
135	    # Extract citation keys from content
136	    citation_ids = list(set(re.findall(r"\[SRC-(\d{3})\]", content)))
137	    citation_ids = [f"SRC-{cid}" for cid in sorted(citation_ids)]
138	
139	    # Evidence IDs used
140	    ev_ids = [ev.get("evidence_id", "") for ev in evidence]
141	
142	    section: ReportSection = {
143	        "section_id": spec.section_id,
144	        "title": spec.title,
145	        "content": content,
146	        "word_count": len(content.split()),
147	        "citation_ids": citation_ids,
148	        "evidence_ids": ev_ids,
149	    }
150	
151	    logger.info(
152	        "Section '%s': %d words, %d citations, %d evidence",
153	        spec.title, section["word_count"], len(citation_ids), len(ev_ids),
154	    )
155	
156	    return {spec.section_id: section}
157	
158	
159	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
160	# Evidence prompt builder
161	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
162	
163	def _build_evidence_prompt(
164	    grouped: dict[str, list[dict[str, Any]]],
165	    registry: SourceRegistry,
166	) -> str:
167	    """Build evidence text grouped by source (Fix R1-#4: no metadata repetition).
168	
169	    Instead of repeating title+abstract for every chunk from the same paper,
170	    prints source metadata ONCE, followed by all chunks from that source.
171	    """
172	    parts: list[str] = []
173	
174	    for url, chunks in grouped.items():
175	        if not chunks:
176	            continue
177	
178	        # Source header — printed ONCE
179	        first = chunks[0]
180	        src_entry = registry.get_by_url(url)
181	        citation_key = first.get("citation_key", "")
182	        title = first.get("source_title", "Unknown")
183	        source_type = first.get("source_type", "web")
184	
185	        parts.append(f"### Source: {title} [{citation_key}]")
186	        parts.append(f"Type: {source_type} | URL: {url[:80]}")
187	        parts.append("")
188	
189	        # Chunks from this source
190	        for i, chunk in enumerate(chunks, 1):
191	            tier = chunk.get("quality_tier", "")
192	            relevance = chunk.get("relevance_score", 0.0)
193	            text = chunk.get("direct_quote", "") or chunk.get("statement", "")
194	            is_table = chunk.get("is_table", False)
195	
196	            if is_table:
197	                parts.append(f"**[Table Data — {tier}]**")
198	            else:
199	                parts.append(f"**[Chunk {i} — {tier}, relevance={relevance:.2f}]**")
200	            parts.append(text)
201	            parts.append("")
202	
203	        parts.append("---")
204	        parts.append("")
205	
206	    return "\n".join(parts)
207	
208	
209	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
210	# Fallback / placeholder sections
211	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
212	
213	def _placeholder_section(spec: SectionSpec) -> ReportSection:
214	    """Build a placeholder section for zero-evidence specs (Fix R3-#2)."""
215	    return {
216	        "section_id": spec.section_id,
217	        "title": spec.title,
218	        "content": EMPTY_SECTION_PLACEHOLDER,
219	        "word_count": 0,
220	        "citation_ids": [],
221	        "evidence_ids": [],
222	    }
223	
224	
225	def _fallback_section(spec: SectionSpec, error: str) -> ReportSection:
226	    """Build a safe fallback section when LLM fails (Fix R6-#4).
227	
228	    This prevents a single section failure from crashing all 15 parallel
229	    writers via asyncio.gather cancellation.
230	    """
231	    content = (
232	        f"> [!WARNING]\n"
233	        f"> This section could not be generated due to a processing error.\n"
234	        f"> Error: {error[:200]}\n"
235	    )
236	    return {
237	        "section_id": spec.section_id,
238	        "title": spec.title,
239	        "content": content,
240	        "word_count": 0,
241	        "citation_ids": [],
242	        "evidence_ids": [],
243	    }
244
